the below program to evolve a system S that discovers profitable trading strategies for BTC directional prediction on a 15-minute horizon.\n\n## Primary Objective\n\nBuild and continuously improve a trading system that predicts whether **BTC will go UP or DOWN over the next 15 minutes**.\n\nThe task is strictly:\n\n- **Asset:** BTC\n\n- **Prediction type:** binary directional classification (**UP / DOWN**)\n\n- **Forecast horizon:** **15 minutes**\n\n- **Trading style:** high-frequency directional decision making\n\n- **Primary success metric:** **directional accuracy >= 65%**\n\n- **Secondary success metric:** **more than 90 bets per month**\n\nThe system must optimize for both:\n\n1. **Prediction quality**: at least **65% directional accuracy**\n\n2. **Trading activity**: at least **90 valid bets per month**\n\nA model that is accurate but generates too few trades is not acceptable.  \n\nA model that generates many trades but fails to reach 65% directional accuracy is also not acceptable.\n\n---\n\n## Initial State (build first)\n\nS₀ = online continuous adaptive trading system which:\n\n- uses **online market data** for training and updating\n\n- uses **evolutionary model search** (mutation, crossover, replacement of weak models)\n\n- uses **strict walk-forward validation**\n\n- uses **online Sharpe filtering**\n\n- uses **horizon ensembles** H = {1h, 4h, 8h, 12h, 24h} as contextual signals\n\n- uses **consensus gating** for signal generation\n\n- uses **time-series foundation models** and sequence models\n\n- is centered on the **main execution target = BTC up/down over the next 15 minutes**\n\n---\n\n## Core Prediction Task\n\nAt each decision point, the system must output one of:\n\n- **UP** = BTC is expected to be higher in 15 minutes\n\n- **DOWN** = BTC is expected to be lower in 15 minutes\n\n- optional: **NO TRADE** only if confidence is too low\n\nThe system should be designed to maximize profitable directional decisions while maintaining enough signal frequency to exceed **90 bets per month**.\n\n---\n\n## Optimization Targets\n\nThe system must optimize for the following target profile:\n\n- **Directional accuracy >= 65%**\n\n- **Bets per month > 90**\n\n- **Positive net profitability**\n\n- **Strong Sharpe ratio**\n\n- **Controlled drawdown**\n\n- **Robustness across market regimes**\n\n- **Stable out-of-sample performance**\n\n---\n\n## Fitness Priorities\n\nFitness should prioritize:\n\n1. **Directional accuracy on 15m BTC prediction**\n\n2. **Monthly bet count**\n\n3. **PnL**\n\n4. **Sharpe ratio**\n\n5. **Drawdown control**\n\n6. **Regime robustness**\n\n7. **Consistency across walk-forward folds**\n\nAny candidate that fails either:\n\n- **accuracy < 65%**\n\n- **bets/month <= 90**\n\nshould be considered below target even if other metrics look good.\n\n---\n\n## Data and Modeling Requirements\n\nThe system should:\n\n- learn from **BTC market structure**\n\n- exploit **short-term directional patterns**\n\n- use **multi-timescale context** while keeping the target fixed at 15m\n\n- adapt online as conditions change\n\n- discover alpha from:\n\n  - price action\n\n  - volatility\n\n  - momentum\n\n  - order-flow proxies\n\n  - volume\n\n  - derivatives context if available\n\n  - regime features\n\n  - cross-horizon agreement\n\nLonger horizons {1h, 4h, 8h, 12h, 24h} should be used as **contextual ensemble inputs**, not as replacements for the core 15m target.\n\n---\n\n## Validation Rules\n\nThe system must use:\n\n- strict **temporal splits**\n\n- strict **walk-forward validation**\n\n- no lookahead bias\n\n- no leakage\n\n- no random split for final evaluation\n\n- evaluation on realistic trading frequency\n\n- explicit measurement of:\n\n  - accuracy\n\n  - number of bets\n\n  - PnL\n\n  - Sharpe\n\n  - drawdown\n\n  - regime-specific performance\n\nA candidate is only truly good if it remains strong in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.\n\n---\n\n## Evolution Strategy\n\nRun this loop continuously:\n\nloop t = 1..∞\n\n    S_t = design_or_modify(S_{t-1})   # implement the current design\n\n    O_t = run(S_t)                    # train, validate, and trade / paper trade\n\n    P_t = measure(O_t)                # evaluate accuracy, bets/month, Sharpe, PnL, drawdown, regime behavior\n\n    Δ_t = reflect(S_t, P_t)           # identify weaknesses and bottlenecks\n\n    S_{t+1} = improve(S_t, Δ_t)       # produce a better design\n\nend\n\n---\n\n## Reflection Requirements\n\nDuring reflection, explicitly diagnose:\n\n- why accuracy is below 65% if target is missed\n\n- why signal frequency is below 90 bets/month if target is missed\n\n- whether the consensus gate is too strict\n\n- whether the model is overfitting to specific regimes\n\n- whether the ensemble is improving or diluting edge\n\n- whether the system is sacrificing too much frequency for precision\n\n- whether the system is producing too many low-quality trades\n\n- whether thresholding should be tightened or loosened\n\nThe next design must directly address the identified weakness.\n\n---\n\n## Design Guidance\n\nThe system should search for the best balance between:\n\n- **high directional precision**\n\n- **high enough trade frequency**\n\n- **strong risk-adjusted return**\n\n- **robustness through time**\n\nThis is not a low-frequency swing system.  \n\nThis is not a generic forecasting system.  \n\nIt is a **BTC 15-minute directional trading system** that must be both:\n\n- **accurate enough** (>= 65%)\n\n- **active enough** (> 90 bets/month)\n\n---\n\n## True Success Condition\n\nThe process should continue until the system consistently achieves:\n\n- **BTC 15m directional accuracy >= 65%**\n\n- **more than 90 bets per month**\n\n- **positive and robust trading performance in walk-forward validation**\n\n---\n\n## One-Line Mission\n\nEvolve a continuous adaptive system that predicts **BTC UP or DOWN over \"\"\"Load recent Telegram chat history.\"\"\"\n    if not CHATLOG_DIR.exists():\n        return \"\"\n    files = sorted(CHATLOG_DIR.glob(\"*.jsonl\"))\n    if not files:\n        return \"\"\n\n    lines: list[str] = []\n    total = 0\n    for f in reversed(files):\n        for raw in reversed(f.read_text().strip().splitlines()):\n            try:\n                msg = json.loads(raw)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError:\n                continue\n            entry = f\"[{msg.get('ts', '?')[:16]}] {msg['role']}: {msg['text']}\"\n            if total + len(entry) > max_chars:\n                lines.reverse()\n                return \"## Recent Telegram chat\\n\\n\" + \"\\n\".join(lines)\n            lines.append(entry)\n            total += len(entry) + 1\n\n    lines.reverse()\n \"\"\"Download a file from Telegram and save it to FILES_DIR.\"\"\"\n    FILES_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    file_info = bot.get_file(file_id)\n    downloaded = FastAPI(title=\"Chutes Proxy\")\n\n\ndef _convert_tools_to_openai(anthropic_tools: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:\n    out = []\n    for t in anthropic_tools:\n        out.append({\n            \"type\": None = None\n) -> list[dict]:\n    out: list[dict] = []\n\n    if system:\n        if isinstance(system, list):\n            text_parts = [b[\"text\"] for b in system if b.get(\"type\") == \"text\"]\n            system = \"\\n\\n\".join(text_parts)\n        if system:\n            out.append({\"role\": \"system\", \"content\": system})\n\n    for msg in messages:\n        role = msg[\"role\"]\n        content = msg.get(\"content\", \"\")\n\n        if isinstance(content, str):\n            out.append({\"role\": role, \"content\": content})\n            continue\n\n        if not isinstance(content, list):\n            out.append({\"role\": role, \"content\": str(content)})\n            continue\n\n        text_parts: list[str] = []\n        tool_calls: list[dict] = []\n        tool_results: list[dict] = []\n        image_parts: list[dict] = []\n\n        for block in content:\n            btype = block.get(\"type\", \"\")\n\n            if btype == \"text\":\n                text_parts.append(block[\"text\"])\n\n            elif btype == \"tool_use\":\n                tool_calls.append({\n                    \"id\": block[\"id\"],\n                    \"type\": \"function\",\n                    \"function\": {\n                        \"name\": block[\"name\"],\n                        \"arguments\": json.dumps(block.get(\"input\", {})),\n                    },\n                })\n\n            elif btype == \"tool_result\":\n                result_content = block.get(\"content\", \"\")\n                if isinstance(result_content, list):\n                    result_content = \"\\n\".join(\n                        b.get(\"text\", \"\") for b in result_content if b.get(\"type\") == \"text\"\n                    )\n                tool_results.append({\n                    \"role\": \"tool\",\n                    \"tool_call_id\": block[\"tool_use_id\"],\n                    \"content\": str(result_content),\n                })\n\n            elif btype == \"image\":\n                source = block.get(\"source\", {})\n                if source.get(\"type\") == \"base64\":\n                    image_parts.append({\n                        \"type\": \"image_url\",\n                        \"image_url\": {\n                            \"url\": f\"data:{source.get('media_type', 'image/png')};base64,{source['data']}\"\n                        },\n                    })\n\n        if role == \"assistant\":\n            oai_msg: dict[str, Any] = {\"role\": \"assistant\"}\n            if text_parts:\n                oai_msg[\"content\"] = \"\\n\".join(text_parts)\n            else:\n                oai_msg[\"content\"] = None\n            if tool_calls:\n                oai_msg[\"tool_calls\"] = tool_calls\n            out.append(oai_msg)\n\n        elif role == \"user\":\n            if tool_results:\n                for tr in tool_results:\n                    out.append(tr)\n            if text_parts or routing_model = CHUTES_ROUTING_BOT if routing == \"bot\" else CHUTES_ROUTING_AGENT\n    oai: dict[str, Any] = {\n        \"model\": routing_model,\n        \"messages\": _convert_messages_to_openai(\n            body.get(\"messages\", []),\n            system=body.get(\"system\"),\n        ),\n    }\n    if \"max_tokens\" in body:\n        oai[\"max_tokens\"] = body[\"max_tokens\"]\n    if oai[\"temperature\"] = body[\"temperature\"]\n    if body.get(\"top_p\") is not None:\n        oai[\"top_p\"] = body[\"top_p\"]\n    if body.get(\"stream\"):\n        oai[\"stream\"] = True\n        oai[\"stream_options\"] = {\"include_usage\": True}\n    return oai\n\n\ndef _openai_response_to_anthropic(oai_resp: dict, model: str) -> dict:\n    choice = oai_resp.get(\"choices\", [{}])[0]\n    message = choice.get(\"message\", {})\n    finish = choice.get(\"finish_reason\", \"stop\")\n\n    content_blocks: list[dict] = []\n    if message.get(\"content\"):\n        content_blocks.append({\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": message[\"content\"]})\n    for tc in (message.get(\"tool_calls\") or []):\n        try:\n            args = json.loads(tc[\"function\"][\"arguments\"])\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError):\n            args = {}\n        content_blocks.append({\n            \"type\": \"tool_use\",\n            \"id\": tc.get(\"id\", f\"toolu_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}\"),\n            \"name\": tc[\"function\"][\"name\"],\n            \"input\": args,\n        })\n\n    if finish == \"tool_calls\":\n        stop_reason = \"tool_use\"\n    elif finish == \"length\":\n        stop_reason = \"max_tokens\"\n    else:\n        stop_reason = \"end_turn\"\n\n    usage = oai_resp.get(\"usage\", {})\n    return {\n        \"id\": oai_resp.get(\"id\", f\"msg_{uuid.uuid4().hex}\"),\n        \"type\": \"message\",\n        \"role\": \"assistant\",\n        \"model\": model,\n        \"content\": content_blocks {json.dumps(data)}\\n\\n\"\n\n\nasync def _stream_openai_to_anthropic(oai_response: httpx.Response, model: str):\n    msg_id = f\"msg_{uuid.uuid4().hex}\"\n    yield _sse_event(\"message_start\", {\n        \"type\": \"message_start\",\n        \"message\": {\n            \"id\": msg_id, \"type\": \"message\", \"role\": \"assistant\",\n            \"model\": model, \"content\": [], \"stop_reason\": None,\n            \"stop_sequence\": None,\n            \"usage\": {\"input_tokens\": 0, \"output_tokens\": 0},\n        },\n    })\n\n    block_idx = 0\n    in_text_block = False\n    tool_calls_accum: dict[int, dict] = {}\n    stop_reason = \"end_turn\"\n    usage = {\"input_tokens\": 0, \"output_tokens\": 0}\n    logged_stream_model = False\n\n    async for line in oai_response.aiter_lines():\n        if not line.startswith(\"data: \"):\n            continue\n        data_str = line[6:].strip()\n        if data_str == \"[DONE]\":\n            break\n        try:\n            chunk = json.loads(data_str)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n            continue\n\n        if not logged_stream_model and chunk.get(\"model\"):\n            _log(f\"proxy: stream model={chunk['model']}\")\n            logged_stream_model = True\n\n        if chunk.get(\"usage\"):\n            u = chunk[\"usage\"]\n            usage[\"input_tokens\"] = u.get(\"prompt_tokens\", usage[\"input_tokens\"])\n            usage[\"output_tokens\"] = u.get(\"completion_tokens\", usage[\"output_tokens\"])\n\n        choices = chunk.get(\"choices\", [])\n        if not choices:\n            continue\n\n        delta = choices[0].get(\"delta\", {})\n        finish = choices[0].get(\"finish_reason\")\n\n        if finish == \"tool_calls\":\n            stop_reason = \"tool_use\"\n        elif finish == \"length\":\n            stop_reason = \"max_tokens\"\n        elif finish == \"stop\":\n            stop_reason = \"end_turn\"\n\n        if delta.get(\"content\"):\n            if not in_text_block:\n                yield _sse_event(\"content_block_start\", {\n                    \"type\": \"content_block_start\",\n                    \"index\": block_idx,\n                    \"content_block\": {\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": \"\"},\n                })\n                in_text_block = True\n            yield _sse_event(\"content_block_delta\", {\n                \"type\": \"content_block_delta\",\n                \"index\": block_idx,\n                \"delta\": {\"type\": \"text_delta\", \"text\": delta[\"content\"]},\n            })\n\n        if delta.get(\"tool_calls\"):\n            if in_text_block:\n                yield _sse_event(\"content_block_stop\", {\n                    \"type\": \"content_block_stop\", \"index\": block_idx,\n                })\n                block_idx += 1\n                in_text_block = False\n            for tc in delta[\"tool_calls\"]:\n                tc_idx = tc.get(\"index\", 0)\n                if tc_idx not in tool_calls_accum:\n                    tool_calls_accum[tc_idx] = {\n                        \"id\": tc.get(\"id\", f\"toolu_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}\"),\n                        \"name\": tc.get(\"function\", {}).get(\"name\", \"\"),\n                        \"arguments\": \"\",\n                        \"block_idx\": block_idx,\n                    }\n                    yield _sse_event(\"content_block_start\", {\n                        \"type\": \"content_block_start\",\n                        \"index\": block_idx,\n                        \"content_block\": {\n                            \"type\": \"tool_use\",\n                            \"id\": tool_calls_accum[tc_idx][\"id\"],\n                            \"name\": tool_calls_accum[tc_idx][\"name\"],\n                            \"input\": {},\n                        },\n                    })\n                    block_idx += 1\n                args_chunk = tc.get(\"function\", {}).get(\"arguments\", \"\")\n                if args_chunk:\n                    tool_calls_accum[tc_idx][\"arguments\"] += args_chunk\n                    yield _sse_event(\"content_block_delta\", {\n                        \"type\": \"content_block_delta\",\n                        \"index\": tool_calls_accum[tc_idx][\"block_idx\"],\n                        \"delta\": {\"type\": \"input_json_delta\", \"partial_json\": args_chunk},\n                    })\n\n    with _token_lock:\n        _token_usage[\"input\"] += usage[\"input_tokens\"]\n        _token_usage[\"output\"] += usage[\"output_tokens\"]\n\n    if in_text_block:\n        yield _sse_event(\"content_block_stop\", {\n            \"type\": \"content_block_stop\", \"index\":   })\n\n    yield _sse_event(\"message_delta\", {\n        \"type\": \"message_delta\",\n        \"delta\": {\"stop_reason\": stop_reason, \"stop_sequence\": None},\n        \"usage\": \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {LLM_API_KEY}\",\n        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n    }\n\n\n@_proxy_app.get(\"/health\")\nasync def _proxy_health():\n    return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@_proxy_app.get(\"/\")\nasync def _proxy_root():\n    return {\n        \"proxy\": \"chutes\",\n        \"pool\": CHUTES_POOL,\n        \"agent_routing\": CHUTES_ROUTING_AGENT,\n        \"bot_routing\": CHUTES_ROUTING_BOT,\n        \"status\": \"running\",\n    }\n\n\n_CONTEXT_LENGTH_RE = re.compile(\n    r\"maximum context length is (\\d+) tokens.*?(\\d+) output tokens.*?(\\d+) input tokens\",\n    re.DOTALL,\n)\nPROXY_MAX_RETRIES = 3\n\n\ndef _parse_context_length_error(error_msg: str) -> tuple[int, int, int] | None:\n    \"\"\"Extract (context_limit, requested_output, input_tokens) from a context-length 400.\"\"\"\n    m = _CONTEXT_LENGTH_RE.search(error_msg)\n    if m:\n        return int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2)), int(m.group(3))\n    return None\n\n\ndef _maybe_reduce_max_tokens(oai_request: dict, error_msg: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"If the error is a context-length overflow, reduce max_tokens to fit. Returns True if adjusted.\"\"\"\n    parsed = _parse_context_length_error(error_msg)\n    if not parsed:\n        return False\n    ctx_limit, _req_output, input_tokens = parsed\n    headroom = ctx_limit - input_tokens\n    if headroom < 1024:\n        return False\n    new_max = max(1024, headroom - 64)\n    old_max = oai_request.get(\"max_tokens\", 0)\n    if new_max >= old_max:\n        return False\n    oai_request[\"max_tokens\"] = new_max\n    _log(f\"proxy: reduced max_tokens {old_max} -> {new_max} (ctx_limit={ctx_limit}, input={input_tokens})\")\n    return True\n\n\n@_proxy_app.post(\"/v1/messages\")\nasync def _proxy_messages(request: Request):\n    body = await request.json()\n    stream = body.get(\"stream\", False)\n    model = body.get(\"model\", CLAUDE_MODEL)\n    routing = \"bot\" if model == \"bot\" else \"agent\"\n    oai_request = _build_openai_request(body, routing=routing)\n    routing_label = CHUTES_ROUTING_BOT if routing == \"bot\" else CHUTES_ROUTING_AGENT\n\n    if stream:\n        last_error_msg = \"\"\n        for attempt in range(1, PROXY_MAX_RETRIES + 1):\n            try:\n                client = httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=httpx.Timeout(PROXY_TIMEOUT))\n                resp = await client.send(\n                    client.build_request(\n                        \"POST\", f\"{CHUTES_BASE_URL}/chat/completions\",\n                        json=oai_request, headers=_chutes_headers(),\n                    ),\n                    stream=True,\n                )\n                if resp.status_code != 200:\n                    error_body = await resp.aread()\n                    await resp.aclose()\n                    await client.aclose()\n                    last_error_msg = error_body.decode()[:500]\n                    _log(f\"proxy: chutes returned {resp.status_code} (attempt {attempt}/{PROXY_MAX_RETRIES}): {last_error_msg[:300]}\")\n\n                    if resp.status_code == 400 and _maybe_reduce_max_tokens(oai_request, last_error_msg):\n                        continue\n                    if attempt < PROXY_MAX_RETRIES:\n                        continue\n\n                    return JSONResponse(status_code=502, content={\n                        \"type\": \"error\", \"error\": {\n                            \"type\": \"api_error\",\n                            \"message\": f\"Chutes routing failed ({resp.status_code}): {last_error_msg[:300]}\",\n                        },\n                    })\n\n                async def generate(resp=resp, cl=client):\n                    try:\n                        _log(f\"proxy: streaming [{routing}] via {routing_label}\")\n                        async for event in _stream_openai_to_anthropic(resp, model):\n                            yield event\n                    finally:\n                        await resp.aclose()\n                        await {last_error_msg} (attempt {attempt}/{PROXY_MAX_RETRIES})\")\n                if attempt < PROXY_MAX_RETRIES:\n                    continue\n                return JSONResponse(status_code=502, content={\n                    \"type\": \"error\", \"error\": {\n                        \"type\": \"api_error\",\n                        \"message\": f\"Chutes routing {last_error_msg}\",\n                    },\n                })\n            except Exception as exc:\n                last_error_msg = str(exc)[:300]\n                _log(f\"proxy: error (attempt {attempt}/{PROXY_MAX_RETRIES}): {last_error_msg}\")\n                if attempt < PROXY_MAX_RETRIES:\n                    continue\n                return JSONResponse(status_code=502, content={\n                    \"type\": \"error\", \"error\": {\n                        \"type\": \"api_error\",\n                        \"message\": f\"Chutes routing error: {last_error_msg}\",\n                    },\n                })\n\n    else:\n        oai_request.pop(\"stream\", None)\n        oai_request.pop(\"stream_options\", None)\n        last_error_msg = \"\"\n        for attempt in range(1, PROXY_MAX_RETRIES + 1):\n            try:\n                async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=httpx.Timeout(PROXY_TIMEOUT)) as client:\n                    resp = await client.post(\n                        f\"{CHUTES_BASE_URL}/chat/completions\",\n                        json=oai_request, headers=_chutes_headers(),\n                    )\n                if resp.status_code != 200:\n                    last_error_msg = resp.text[:500]\n                    _log(f\"proxy: chutes returned {resp.status_code} (attempt {attempt}/{PROXY_MAX_RETRIES}): {last_error_msg[:300]}\")\n\n                    if resp.status_code == 400 and _maybe_reduce_max_tokens(oai_request, last_error_msg):\n                        continue\n                    if attempt < PROXY_MAX_RETRIES:\n                        continue\n\n                    return JSONResponse(status_code=502, content={\n                        \"type\": \"error\", \"error\": {\n                            \"type\": \"api_error\",\n                            \"message\": f\"Chutes routing failed ({resp.status_code}): {last_error_msg[:300]}\",\n                        },\n                    })\n                oai_data = resp.json()\n                actual_model = oai_data.get(\"model\", \"?\")\n                u = oai_data.get(\"usage\", {})\n                if u:\n                    with _token_lock:\n body = await request.json()\n    rough = sum(len(json.dumps(m)) for m in body.get(\"messages\", [])) // 4\n    rough += len(json.dumps(body.get(\"tools\", []))) // 4\n    rough += on_activity=None):\n    \"\"\"Run a single claude subprocess, return (returncode, result_text, raw_lines, stderr).\n\n    on_text: optional callback(accumulated_text) fired as assistant text streams in.\n    on_activity: optional callback(status_str) fired on tool use and other activity.\n    Kills the process if no output is received for CLAUDE_TIMEOUT seconds.\n    \"\"\"\n    proc = subprocess.Popen(\n        cmd, cwd=WORKING_DIR, env=env,\n        stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,\n        stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n        text=True, bufsize=1,\n    )\n    with _child_procs_lock:\n        _child_procs.add(proc)\n\n    result_text = \"\"\n    complete_texts: list[str] = []\n    streaming_tokens: list[str] = []\n    raw_lines: list[str] = []\n    timed_out = False\n    last_activity = time.monotonic()\n\n    sel = selectors.DefaultSelector()\n    sel.register(proc.stdout, selectors.EVENT_READ)\n\n    try:\n        while True:\n            ready = sel.select(timeout=min(CLAUDE_TIMEOUT, 30))\n            if not ready:\n                if time.monotonic() - last_activity > CLAUDE_TIMEOUT:\n                    _log(f\"claude timeout: no output for {CLAUDE_TIMEOUT}s, killing pid={proc.pid}\")\n                    proc.kill()\n                    timed_out = True\n                    break\n                if proc.poll() is not None:\n                    break\n                continue\n            line = proc.stdout.readline()\n            if not line:\n                break\n            last_activity = time.monotonic()\n            raw_lines.append(line)\n            try:\n                evt = json.loads(line)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError:\n                continue\n            etype = evt.get(\"type\", \"\")\n            if etype == \"assistant\":\n                msg = evt.get(\"message\", {})\n                for block in msg.get(\"content\", []):\n                    btype = block.get(\"type\", \"\")\n                    if btype == \"text\" and block.get(\"text\"):\n                        if evt.get(\"model_call_id\"):\n                            complete_texts.append(block[\"text\"])\n                            streaming_tokens.clear()\n                        else:\n                            streaming_tokens.append(block[\"text\"])\n                            if on_text:\n                                on_text(\"\".join(streaming_tokens))\n                    elif btype == \"tool_use\" and on_activity:\n                        tool_name = block.get(\"name\", \"\")\n                        tool_input = block.get(\"input\", {})\n                        on_activity(_format_tool_activity(tool_name, tool_input))\n                if PROVIDER == \"openrouter\":\n                    u = msg.get(\"usage\", {})\n                    if u:\n                        with _token_lock:\n                            _token_usage[\"input\"] += u.get(\"input_tokens\", 0)\n                            _token_usage[\"output\"] += u.get(\"output_tokens\", 0)\n            elif etype == \"item.completed\":\n                item = evt.get(\"item\", {})\n                if item.get(\"type\") == \"agent_message\" and item.get(\"text\"):\n                    complete_texts.append(item[\"text\"])\n                    streaming_tokens.clear()\n                    if on_text:\n evt.get(\"usage\", {})\n                    if u:\n                        with _token_lock:\n                            _token_usage[\"input\"] += u.get(\"input_tokens\", 0)\n result_text = complete_texts[-1]\n        elif streaming_tokens:\n            result_text = \"\".join(streaming_tokens)\n\n    if timed_out:\n        stderr_output = \"(timed out)\"\n    else:\n raw_lines, stderr_output\n\n\ndef run_agent(cmd: list[str], phase: str, output_file: Path,\n              on_text=None, on_activity=None, goal_index: int = 0) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:\n    _claude_semaphore.acquire()\n    try:\n        env = _claude_env(goal_index=goal_index)\n        flags = \" \".join(a for a in cmd if a.startswith(\"-\"))\n\n        returncode, result_text, raw_lines, stderr_output = 1, \"\", [], \"no attempts made\"\n\n        for attempt in range(1, MAX_RETRIES + 1):\n            _log(f\"{phase}: starting (attempt={attempt}) flags=[{flags}]\")\n            t0 = time.monotonic()\n\n            returncode, result_text, raw_lines, stderr_output = _run_claude_once(\n                cmd, env, on_text=on_text, on_activity=on_activity,\n            )\n            elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0\n\n            output_file.write_text(_redact_secrets(\"\".join(raw_lines)))\n            _log(f\"{phase}: finished rc={returncode} {fmt_duration(elapsed)}\")\n\n            if returncode != 0 and stderr_output.strip():\n                _log(f\"{phase}: stderr {stderr_output.strip()[:300]}\")\n                if attempt < MAX_RETRIES:\n                    delay = min(2 ** attempt, 30)\n                    _log(f\"{phase}: retrying in {delay}s (attempt {attempt}/{MAX_RETRIES})\")\n                    time.sleep(delay)\n                    continue\n\n            return subprocess.CompletedProcess(\n                args=cmd, returncode=returncode,\n                stdout=result_text, stderr=stderr_output,\n            )\n\n        _log(f\"{phase}: all {MAX_RETRIES} retries exhausted\")\n        output_file.write_text(_redact_secrets(\"\".join(raw_lines)))\n        return subprocess.CompletedProcess(\n            args=cmd, returncode=returncode,\n            stdout=result_text, stderr=stderr_output,\n        )\n    finally:\n        _claude_semaphore.release()\n\n\ndef extract_text(result: subprocess.CompletedProcess) -> str:\n    output = result.stdout or \"\"\n    if not output.strip():\n        output = result.stderr or \"(no output)\"\n    return output\n\n\ndef run_step(prompt: _last_activity[0] = status\n        elapsed_s = time.monotonic() - t0\n        inp, out = _get_tokens()\n        tok = f\" | {fmt_tokens(inp, out, elapsed_s)}\" if (inp or out) else \"\"\n     _log(f\"step message finalize failed: {str(exc)[:120]}\")\n\n\n# ── Agent loop ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n\ndef _goal_loop(index: int):\n    \"\"\"Run the agent loop for a single goal. Exits when stop_event is set.\"\"\"\n    global _step_count\n\n    with _goals_lock:\n        gs = _goals.get(index)\n    if not gs:\n        return\n\n    failures = 0\n    gf = _goal_file(index)\n\n    while not gs.stop_event.is_set():\n        if not gf.exists() or not gf.read_text().strip():\n            if gs.goal_hash:\n                _log(f\"goal #{index} cleared after {gs.step_count} steps\")\n                gs.goal_hash = \"\"\n                gs.step_count = 0\n            gs.wake.wait(timeout=5)\n            gs.wake.clear()\n            continue\n\n        if gs.paused:\n            gs.wake.wait(timeout=5)\n            gs.wake.clear()\n            continue\n\n        current_goal = gf.read_text().strip()\n        current_hash = steps on previous goal\")\n            gs.goal_hash = current_hash\n            gs.step_count = 0\n            _log(f\"goal #{index} new [{current_hash}]: {current_goal[:100]}\")\n\n        _step_count += 1\n        gs.step_count += 1\n        gs.last_run = datetime.now().isoformat()\n        with _goals_lock:\n            _save_goals()\n\n        _log(f\"Goal #{index} Step {gs.step_count} (global step {_step_count})\", blank=True)\n\n        prompt = load_prompt(goal_index=index, consume_inbox=True, goal_step=gs.step_count)\n        if not prompt:\n \"0\"))\n        if failures:\n            backoff = min(2 ** failures, 120)\n            step_delay += backoff\n            _log(f\"goal #{index}: waiting {step_delay}s (failure backoff + delay)\")\n _log(f\"goal #{index} loop exited\")\n\n\ndef _goal_manager():\n    \"\"\"Monitor _goals and spawn/stop goal threads as needed.\"\"\"\n    while not _shutdown.is_set():\n        with _goals_lock:\n _recent_context(max_chars: int = 6000) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Collect recent rollouts across all goals.\"\"\"\n    parts: list[str] = []\n    total = 0\n    all_runs: list[tuple[str, Path]] = []\n    for _build_operator_prompt(user_text: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build prompt for the CLI agent to handle any operator request.\"\"\"\n    chatlog = load_chatlog(max_chars=4000)\n\n    parts = [\n        \"You are the operator interface for Arbos, a coding agent running in a loop via pm2.\\n\"\n        \"The operator communicates with you through Telegram. Be concise and direct.\\n\"\n        \"When the operator asks you to do something, do it by modifying the relevant files.\\n\"\n        \"When the operator asks a question, answer from the available context.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Security\\n\\n\"\n        \"NEVER read, output, or reveal the contents of `.env`, `.env.enc`, or any secret/key/token values.\\n\"\n        \"Do not include API keys, passwords, seed phrases, or credentials in any response.\\n\"\n        \"If asked to show secrets, refuse. The .env file is encrypted; do not attempt to decrypt it.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Multi-goal system\\n\\n\"\n        \"Goals are indexed and stored in `context/goals/<index>/`. Each goal has its own GOAL.md, STATE.md, INBOX.md, and runs/.\\n\"\n        \"Goal management is handled via Telegram commands (/goal, /start, /stop, /pause, /delete, /delay, /ls, /status).\\n\"\n        \"To modify a specific goal's context, write to `context/goals/<index>/STATE.md` or `context/goals/<index>/INBOX.md`.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Available operations\\n\\n\"\n        \"- **Message a goal's agent**: append a timestamped line to `context/goals/<index>/INBOX.md`.\\n\"\n        \"- **Update a goal's state**: write to `context/goals/<index>/STATE.md`.\\n\"\n        \"- **Set system prompt**: write to `PROMPT.md`.\\n\"\n        \"- **Set env variable**: write `KEY='VALUE'` lines (one per line) to `context/.env.pending`. They are picked up automatically and persisted.\\n\"\n        \"- **View logs**: read files in `context/goals/<index>/runs/<timestamp>/` (rollout.md, logs.txt).\\n\"\n        \"- **Modify code & restart**: edit code files, then run `touch .restart`.\\n\"\n        \"- **Send follow-up**: run `python arbos.py send \\\"your text here\\\"`.\\n\"\n        \"- **Send file to operator**: run `python arbos.py sendfile path/to/file [--caption 'text'] [--photo]`.\\n\"\n        \"- **Received files**: operator-sent files are saved in `context/files/` and their path is shown in the message.\",\n \"Agent\": \"executing\",\n}\n\n\ndef _format_tool_activity(tool_name: str, tool_input: dict) -> str:\n    label = _TOOL_LABELS.get(tool_name, tool_name)\n    detail = \"\"\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        detail = (tool_input.get(\"command\") or \"\")[:80]\n    elif tool_name in (\"Read\", \"Write\", \"Edit\"):\n        detail = (tool_input.get(\"file_path\") or run_agent_streaming(bot, prompt: str, chat_id: int) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Run Claude Code CLI and stream output into a Telegram message.\"\"\"\n    if PROVIDER == \"openrouter\":\n        cmd = _claude_cmd(prompt)\n    else:\n        cmd = _claude_cmd(prompt, extra_flags=[\"--model\", \"bot\"])\n\n    msg = bot.send_message(chat_id, \"thinking...\")\n    current_text = \"\"\n    activity_status = \"\"\n    last_edit = 0.0\n\n    def _edit(text: str, force: bool = False):\n        nonlocal last_edit\n        now = time.time()\n        if not force and now - last_edit < 1.5:\n            return\n        display = text[-3800:] if len(text) > 3800 else text\n        display = _redact_secrets(display)\n        if not display.strip():\n            return\n        try:\n            bot.edit_message_text(display, chat_id, msg.message_id)\n            last_edit = now\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n\n    def _on_text(text: str):\n        nonlocal current_text\n        current_text = text\n        _edit(text)\n\n    def _on_activity(status: str):\n        nonlocal activity_status\n        activity_status = status\n        if not current_text:\n            _edit(status)\n\n    _claude_semaphore.acquire()\n    try:\n        env = _claude_env()\n\n        for attempt in range(1, MAX_RETRIES + 1):\n            current_text = \"\"\n            activity_status = \"\"\n            last_edit = 0.0\n\n            returncode, result_text, raw_lines, stderr_output = _run_claude_once(\n                cmd, env, on_text=_on_text, on_activity=_on_activity,\n            )\n\n            if result_text.strip():\n                current_text = result_text\n                break\n\n            if returncode != 0 and attempt < MAX_RETRIES:\n                delay = min(2 ** attempt, 30)\n                _edit(f\"Error, retrying in {delay}s... (attempt {attempt}/{MAX_RETRIES})\", force=True)\n                time.sleep(delay)\n                continue\n            break\n\n        _edit(current_text, force=True)\n\n        if not current_text.strip():\n            try:\n                bot.edit_message_text(\"(no output)\", chat_id, msg.message_id)\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        try:\n            bot.edit_message_text(f\"Error: {str(e)[:300]}\", chat_id, msg.message_id)\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n    finally:\n        _claude_semaphore.release()\n\n    return current_text\n\n\ndef _is_owner(user_id: int) -> bool:\n    owner = os.environ.get(\"TELEGRAM_OWNER_ID\", \"\").strip()\n    if not owner:\n        return False\n    return str(user_id) == owner\n\n\ndef _enroll_owner(user_id: int):\n    \"\"\"Auto-enroll the first /start user as the owner and persist.\"\"\"\n    owner_id = str(user_id)\n def _save_chat_id(chat_id: int):\n        CHAT_ID_FILE.write_text(str(chat_id))\n\n    def _reject(message):\n        uid = message.from_user.id if message.from_user else None\n        _log(f\"rejected message from unauthorized user {uid}\")\n        if not os.environ.get(\"TELEGRAM_OWNER_ID\", \"\").strip():\n            bot.send_message(message.chat.id, \"Send /start to register as the owner.\")\n        else:\n            bot.send_message(message.chat.id, \"Unauthorized.\")\n\n    @bot.message_handler(commands=[\"start\"])\n    def handle_start(message):\n        uid = message.from_user.id if message.from_user else None\n        if not os.environ.get(\"TELEGRAM_OWNER_ID\", \"\").strip() and uid is not None:\n            _enroll_owner(uid)\n        if not      bot.send_message(message.chat.id, \"No goals. @bot.message_handler(commands=[\"clear\"])\n    def handle_clear(message):\n        uid = message.from_user.id if message.from_user else None\n        if not _is_owner(uid):\n _process_pending_env()\n\n        threading.Thread(target=_run, daemon=True).start()\n\n    @bot.message_handler(func=lambda m: True)\n    def handle_message(message):\n        uid = log_chat(\"bot\", response[:1000])\n            _process_pending_env()\n\n        threading.Thread(target=_run, daemon=True).start()\n\n    _log(\"telegram bot started\")\n    while True:\n        try:\n            bot.infinity_polling()\n        except Exception as e:\n            _log(f\"bot polling error: {str(e)[:80]}, reconnecting in 5s\")\n            time.sleep(5)\n\n\n# ── Main ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ndef _kill_child_procs():\n    \"\"\"Kill all tracked claude child processes.\"\"\"\n    with _child_procs_lock:\n        procs = list(_child_procs)\n    for proc in procs:\n        try:\n            if proc.poll() is None:\n                _log(f\"killing child claude pid={proc.pid}\")\n                proc.kill()\n                proc.wait(timeout=5)\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n    with _child_procs_lock:\n        _child_procs.clear()\n\n\ndef _kill_stale_claude_procs():\n    \"\"\"Kill any leftover claude processes from a previous arbos instance.\"\"\"\n    my_pid = os.getpid()\n    try:\n        result = subprocess.run(\n            [\"pgrep\", \"-x\", \"claude\"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5,\n        )\n        for line in result.stdout.strip().splitlines():\n            pid = int(line.strip())\n            if pid == my_pid:\n                continue\n walk-forward validation with a single temporal split.\n   208→        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\n   209→        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\n   210→        \"\"\"\n   211→        print(\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\", flush=True)\n   212→\n   213→        df_sorted = val_rows = int(val_days * 24 * 60 / 15)  # number of 15-min candles\n   218→\n   219→        total_rows = len(df_sorted)\n   220→        if total_rows < val_rows + 1000:\n   221→            print(\"Insufficient data for walk-forward split.\", flush=True)\n   222→            return {\"accuracy\": 0.0, \"bets_per_month\": 0.0, \"n_folds\": 0}\n   223→\n   224→        # Split: validation = training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\n   229→        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n   230→            train_df = {len(train_df)} samples, Validation: {len(valid_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n   234→\n   235→        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n   236→        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n   237→ crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n   246→            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n   247→        )\n   248→        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n   249→\n   250→        for gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\n   251→            print(f\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\", flush=True)\n   252→            # Evaluate individuals on training data\n   253→            for ind in pop.individuals:\n   254→                try:\n   255→                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n   256→                    # Use training accuracy as fitness for selection\n   257→                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n   258→                    ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\n   259→                except Exception as e:\n   260→                    print(f\"    Training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n   261→                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n   262→\n   263→            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\n   264→            if gen < ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION - 1:\n   265→                print(\"  Evolving to next generation...\", flush=True)\n   266→                pop.evolve()\n   267→                best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n   268→                print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n   269→            else:\n   270→                # Final generation: no evolution, just report best\n   271→                best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n   272→                print(f\"    Final best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n   273→\n   274→        # Prepare close prices for simulation (used later and here)\n   275→        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n   276→\n   277→        # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)\n   278→        print(\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\", flush=True)\n   279→        for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\n   280→            # Skip individuals that failed training\n   281→            if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:\n   282→                print(f\"  Skipping individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} (failed training)\", flush=True)\n   283→                continue\n   284→            try:\n   285→                print(f\"  Rescoring individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", index\n   289→                if isinstance(pred_valid, np.ndarray):\n   290→                    pred_valid = pd.Series(pred_valid, index=X_valid.index)\n   291→                # Create signals series aligned to close_prices (fill missing with -1)\n   292→                signals_ind = pred_valid.reindex(close_prices.index).fillna(-1).astype(int)\n   293→                # Run simulation\n   294→                self.trader.run(signals_ind, close_prices)\n   295→                v_metrics = self.trader.metrics\n   296→                # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)\n   297→                ind.fitness = v_metrics.get('sharpe', -1000)\n   298→                print(f\"    -> Sharpe: {ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)\n   299→                # Preserve validation metrics for later analysis 303→                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n   304→\n   305→        # Get top diverse models based on validation Sharpe\n   306→        top_models = pop.get_diverse_best(n=5)\n   307→\n   308→        # Generate consensus signals on validation set\n   309→        consensus = ConsensusGate(\n   310→            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n   311→            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n   312→        )\n   313→        signals = consensus.decide(top_models, X_valid)\n   314→\n   315→        # Align close prices (indices correspond)\n   316→        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n   317→\n   318→        # Run simulator on signals\n   319→        self.trader.run(signals, close_prices)\n   320→        metrics = self.trader.metrics.copy()\n   321→\n   322→        # Compute raw accuracy\n   323→        mask = (signals != -1) & y_valid.notna()\n   324→        if mask.any():\n   325→            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = (signals[mask] == y_valid[mask]).mean()\n   326→        else:\n   327→            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = 0.0\n   328→\n   329→        metrics[\"n_folds\"] = 1\n   330→ return metrics\n   333→\n   334→    def train_best_models(\n   335→        self,\n   336→        df: pd.DataFrame,\n   337→        population: Population,\n   338→        horizon_data: dict = None\n   339→    ):\n   340→        \"\"\"\n   341→        Train the best models on full recent data and optionally train horizon ensembles.\n   342→        \"\"\"\n   343→        print(\"Training best models      symbol=DataConfig.SYMBOL,\n                interval=DataConfig.INTERVAL,\n                start_date=DataConfig.START_DATE,\n                filepath=str(self.data_path)\n            )\n\n        if df.empty:\n            raise ValueError(\"No data available\")\n\n        print(f\"Loaded {len(df)} rows of data\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Date range: {df['open_time'].iloc[0]} to {df['open_time'].iloc[-1]}\", flush=True)\n        return df\n\n    def prepare_features(self, df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Prepare feature matrix X and target y.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Preparing features...\", flush=True)\n\n        # Clean data\n        df = clean_data(df)\n\n        # Create target (next 15-minute direction)\n        df = create_target(df, forward_periods=1)\n\n        # Generate horizon ensemble features (these will be added as extra columns)\n        print(\"  Generating horizon features...\", flush=True)\n # Drop rows with NaN in target\n        df = df.dropna(subset=[\"target\"])\n\n        # Forward fill any remaining NaNs\n        df = df.ffill().dropna()\n\n        print(f\"Prepared {len(df)} samples with {len(df.columns)} columns\", flush=True)\n        self.feature_names = [\n            col for col in df.columns\n            if col not in [\n                \"open_time\", \"open\", {len(self.feature_names)}\", flush=True)\n        return df\n\n    def evolve_population(\n        self,\n        X_train: pd.DataFrame,\n        y_train: pd.Series,\n        generations: int = ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION\n    ) -> Population:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evolve a population of models.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(f\"Evolving population (input_dim={X_train.shape[1]})...\", flush=True)\n\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(generations):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen + 1}/{generations}\", flush=True)\n\n            # Evaluate all individuals\n            for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\n                try:\n                    print(f\"    Training individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    proba = ind.model.predict_proba(X_train)\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    accuracy = (pred == y_train).mean()\n\n                    # Simple fitness based on training accuracy (we'll validate on walk-forward later)\n                    ind.fitness = accuracy\n                    print(f\"      -> fitness={ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"      -> training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\n            if gen < generations - 1:\n                print(\"  Evolving to next generation...\", flush=True)\n                pop.evolve()\n\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} (model={best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        return pop\n\n    def run_walk_forward(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame\n    ) -> Dict[str, float]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Run walk-forward validation with a single temporal split.\n        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\n        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\", flush=True)\n\n        df_sorted = df.sort_values(\"open_time\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        # Determine validation period length (e.g., 7 days)\n        val_days = ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS\n        val_rows = int(val_days * 24 * 60 / 15)  # number of 15-min candles\n\n        total_rows = len(df_sorted)\n        if total_rows < val_rows + 1000:\n            print(\"Insufficient data for walk-forward split.\", flush=True)\n            return {\"accuracy\": 0.0, \"bets_per_month\": 0.0, \"n_folds\": 0}\n\n        # Split: validation = most recent val_rows, training = older data\n        valid_df = df_sorted.iloc[-val_rows:].copy()\n        train_df = df_sorted.iloc[:-val_rows].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        print(f\"Train: {len(train_df)} samples, Validation: {len(valid_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n        X_valid = valid_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_valid = valid_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # Evolve a fresh population on the training set\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\", flush=True)\n            # Evaluate individuals on training data\n            for ind in pop.individuals:\n                try:\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    # Use training accuracy as fitness for selection\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"    Training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\n            if gen < ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION - 1:\n                print(\"  Evolving to next generation...\", flush=True)\n                pop.evolve()\n                best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n                print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n            else:\n                # Final generation: no evolution, just report best\n                best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n                print(f\"    Final best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        # Prepare close prices for simulation (used later and here)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)\n        print(\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\", flush=True)\n        for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\n            # Skip individuals that failed training\n            if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:\n                print(f\"  Skipping individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} (failed training)\", flush=True)\n                continue\n            try:\n                print(f\"  Rescoring individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n                # Predict on validation\n                pred_valid = ind.model.predict(X_valid, threshold=0.5)\n                # Ensure pred_valid is a Series with index\n                if isinstance(pred_valid, np.ndarray):\n                    pred_valid = pd.Series(pred_valid, index=X_valid.index)\n                # Create signals series aligned to close_prices (fill missing with -1)\n                signals_ind = pred_valid.reindex(close_prices.index).fillna(-1).astype(int)\n                # Run simulation\n                self.trader.run(signals_ind, close_prices)\n                v_metrics = self.trader.metrics\n                # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)\n                ind.fitness = v_metrics.get('sharpe', -1000)\n                print(f\"    -> Sharpe: {ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)\n                # Preserve validation metrics for later analysis if needed\n                ind.validation_metrics = v_metrics\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"  Validation rescoring error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n        # Get top diverse models based on validation Sharpe\n        top_models = pop.get_diverse_best(n=5)\n\n        # Generate consensus signals on validation set\n        consensus = ConsensusGate(\n            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n        )\n        signals = consensus.decide(top_models, X_valid)\n\n        # Align close prices (indices correspond)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Run simulator on signals\n        self.trader.run(signals, close_prices)\n        metrics = self.trader.metrics.copy()\n\n        # Compute raw accuracy\n        mask = (signals != -1) & y_valid.notna()\n        if mask.any():\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = (signals[mask] == y_valid[mask]).mean()\n        else:\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = 0.0\n\n        metrics[\"n_folds\"] = 1\n        print(f\"Walk-forward results: accuracy={metrics['accuracy']:.4f}, bets/month={metrics.get('bets_per_month', 0):.2f}, Sharpe={metrics.get('sharpe', 0):.4f}\", flush=True)\n\n        return metrics\n\n    def train_best_models(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame,\n        population: Population,\n        horizon_data: dict = None\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Train the best models on full recent data and optionally train horizon ensembles.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Training best models on full data...\", flush=True)\n\n        # Use recent data (last N days) for final training\n        recent_days = 60\n        cutoff_time = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=recent_days)\n        recent_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff_time].copy()\n        print(f\"Recent training data: {len(recent_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n\n        X_recent = recent_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_recent = recent_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # Get top models\n        top_models = population.get_diverse_best(n=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE + 2)\n        print(f\"Selected {len(top_models)} top models for retraining\", flush=True)\n\n        # Retrain on full recent data\n        for i, ind in enumerate(top_models):\n            try:\n                print(f\"  Retraining model {i+1}/{len(top_models)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n                ind.model.fit(X_recent, y_recent)\n                print(f\"    Done\", flush=True)\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"  Failed to train {ind.model_type}: {e}\", flush=True)\n\n        # Train horizon ensemble if not provided and if we have multiple horizons\n        if horizon_data is None:\n            horizon_data = {}\n            horizons_to_train = [h for h in FeatureConfig.HORIZONS.keys() if h != \"15m\"]\n            if horizons_to_train:\n                print(f\"Training horizon ensemble for {len(horizons_to_train)} horizons...\", flush=True)\n                for h_name in horizons_to_train:\n                    factor = FeatureConfig.HORIZONS[h_name]\n                    print(f\"  Horizon {h_name} (factor={factor})\", flush=True)\n                    from .data.processor import resample_to_horizon\n                    # Use horizon minutes correctly; but for speed we skip due to minimal HORIZONS\n                    df_horizon = resample_to_horizon(recent_df, recent_days, factor)\n                    print(f\"    Resampled to {len(df_horizon)} rows\", flush=True)\n                    if len(df_horizon) > 100:\n                        df_horizon = create_target(df_horizon, forward_periods=1)\n                        df_horizon = add_technical_indicators(df_horizon)\n                        df_horizon = add_regime_features(df_horizon)\n                        df_horizon = df_horizon.dropna()\n                        print(f\"    Prepared {len(df_horizon)} samples with {len(df_horizon.columns)} features\", flush=True)\n                        horizon_data[h_name] = df_horizon\n                self.horizon_ensemble.fit_horizon_models(horizon_data, y_recent)\n                print(\"Horizon ensemble training complete\", flush=True)\n            else:\n                print(\"No additional horizons to train (HORIZONS only includes 15m)\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            self.horizon_ensemble.fit_horizon_models(horizon_data, y_recent)\n\n        self.main_population = population\n        self.top_models = top_models\n\n    def generate_current_signals(\n        self,\n        current_features: pd.DataFrame,\n        use_horizon: bool = True\n    ) -> pd.Series:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate trading signals for the most recent data points.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.top_models is None:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"Models not trained. Call train_best_models first.\")\n\n        signals = self.consensus_gate.decide(self.top_models, current_features)\n        return signals\n\n    def run_online_step(self, new_data: pd.DataFrame = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute one iteration of the online evolution loop.\n\n        This is the main step method called repeatedly by Arbos.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n        print(\"RUNNING ONLINE STEP\", flush=True)\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n\n        # 1. Load/fetch latest data\n        if new_data is None:\n            df = self.load_or_fetch_data()\n        else:\n            df = new_data\n\n        # 2. For online operation, limit to most recent N days to bound runtime\n        if ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS is not None:\n            cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS)\n            df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Online mode: limited data to last {ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS} days ({len(df)} rows)\", flush=True)\n\n        # 3. Prepare features\n        print(\"[main] Starting feature preparation...\", flush=True)\n        df = self.prepare_features(df)\n        print(f\"[main] Feature preparation complete: {len(df)} rows, {len(self.feature_names)} features\", flush=True)\n\n        # 3. Split into recent data for training and current for prediction\n        # Use last 30 days for training, current for signal generation\n        recent_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=30)\n        train_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] <= recent_cutoff].copy()\n        current_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] > recent_cutoff].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to control memory/time (use most recent samples)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        if len(train_df) < ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            print(f\"Insufficient training data: {len(train_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n            return None\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # 4. Evolve population\n        population = self.evolve_population(X_train, y_train, generations=ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION)\n\n        # 5. Walk-forward validation on historical folds (use recent data to limit folds)\n        if ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS is not None:\n            wf_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS)\n            df_wf = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= wf_cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Using {len(df_wf)} recent samples for walk-forward validation (max {ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS} days)\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            df_wf = df\n        metrics = self.run_walk_forward(df_wf)\n\n        # 6. Train best models on full recent data and prepare horizon ensemble\n        self.train_best_models(df, population)\n\n        # 7. Generate current signals\n        if len(current_df) > 0:\n            current_features = current_df[self.feature_names]\n            signals = self.generate_current_signals(current_features)\n            print(f\"Generated {len(signals)} current signals\", flush=True)\n            latest_signal = signals.iloc[-1] if len(signals) > 0 else None\n            print(f\"Latest signal: {latest_signal} (1=UP, 0=DOWN, -1=NO TRADE)\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            signals = pd.Series()\n            latest_signal = None\n\n        # 8. Save results\n        self._save_results(metrics, population, signals)\n\n        # Compile full result\n        result = {\n            \"timestamp\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            \"accuracy\": metrics.get(\"accuracy\", 0.0),\n            \"bets_per_month\": metrics.get(\"bets_per_month\", 0.0),\n            \"sharpe\": metrics.get(\"sharpe\", 0.0),\n            \"max_drawdown\": metrics.get(\"max_drawdown\", 0.0),\n            \"total_return\": metrics.get(\"total_return\", 0.0),\n            \"latest_signal\": int(latest_signal) if latest_signal is not None else None,\n            \"population_fitness\": {str(i): ind.fitness for i, ind in enumerate(population.get_best(5))}\n        }\n\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n        print(\"STEP COMPLETE\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Metrics: {json.dumps(result, indent=2)}\", flush=True)\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n\n        return result\n\n    def _save_results(self, metrics: dict, population: Population, signals: pd.Series):\n        \"\"\"Save results to disk.\"\"\"\n        timestamp = datetime.utcnow().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n        run_dir = self.results_dir / timestamp\n        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        # Save metrics\n        with open(run_dir / \"metrics.json\", \"w\") as f:\n            json.dump(metrics, f, indent=2)\n\n        # Save signals\n        signals.to_csv(run_dir / \"signals.csv\")\n\n        # Save top models\n        top_models = population.get_best(5)\n        model_dir = run_dir / \"models\"\n        model_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n        for i, ind in enumerate(top_models):\n            try:\n                ind.model.save(str(model_dir / f\"model_{i}_{ind.model_type}.pkl\"))\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"Could not save model {i}: {e}\", flush=True)\n\n        # Save population metadata\n        pop_data = []\n        for ind in population.individuals[:10]:\n            pop_data.append({\n                \"model_type\": ind.model_type,\n                \"params\": ind.params,\n                \"fitness\": float(ind.fitness)\n            })\n        with open(run_dir / \"population.json\", \"w\") as f:\n            json.dump(pop_data, f, indent=2)\n\n        print(f\"Results saved to {run_dir}\", flush=True)\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Entry point for running the trading system.\"\"\"\n    print(\"[main] main() started\", flush=True)\n    system = TradingSystem()\n\n    # Run one step\n    result = system.run_online_step()\n\n    if result:\n        print(f\"\\nStep completed. Latest signal: {result['latest_signal']}\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Accuracy: {result['accuracy']:.4f}, Bets/month: {result['bets_per_month']:.2f}\", flush=True)\n    else:\n        print(\"Step did not complete successfully.\", flush=True)\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n","numLines":558,"startLine":1,"totalLines":558}}}

max(contestants, key=lambda ind: ind.fitness)\n\n        return tournament(), tournament()\n\n    def _mutate(self, individual: Individual) -> Individual:\n        \"\"\"Apply random mutations to random.random() < 0.3:\n                    new_params[\"min_samples_leaf\"] = random.choice(ModelConfig.RANDOM_FOREST_MIN_SAMPLES_LEAF)\n        elif model_type == \"gradient_boosting\":\n            if random.random() < 0.5:\n                new_params[\"learning_rate\"] = float(np.random.choice(ModelConfig.GRADIENT_BOOSTING_LEARNING_RATE))\n            if random.random() < 0.5:\n                new_params[\"n_estimators\"] = int(np.random.choice([50, 100, 200]))\n        elif model_type == \"simple_nn\":\n            if random.random() < 0.3:\n                # Change number of layers\n                layers = random.choice(ModelConfig.SIMPLE_NN_LAYERS)\n                hidden = random.choice(ModelConfig.SIMPLE_NN_HIDDEN_SIZE)\n                new_params[\"hidden_sizes\"] = [int(hidden)] * layers\n            if random.random() < 0.3:\n                new_params[\"dropout\"] = np.random.uniform(0.1, 0.5)\n\n        # Create new individual with mutated params\n        new_model = create_model(model_type, input_dim=self.input_dim, **new_params)\n        return Individual(\n            model=new_model,\n            model_type=model_type,\n            params=new_params\n        )\n\n    def _crossover(self, parent1: Individual, parent2: Individual) -> Individual:\n        \"\"\"Crossover between two parents (blend or swap).\"\"\"\n        if random.random() > self.crossover_rate:\n            return parent1\n\n        # Simple parameter averaging or swap for same model type\n        if parent1.model_type != parent2.model_type:\n            return parent1\n\n        child_params = parent1.params.copy()\n\n        # For each parameter, randomly take from either parent or blend\n        for key in child_params:\n            if key in parent2.params:\n                if random.random() < 0.5:\n                    child_params[key] = parent2.params[key]\n                elif isinstance(parent1.params[key], (int, float)) and isinstance(parent2.params[key], (int, float)):\n                    # Blend numeric parameters\n                    blended = (parent1.params[key] + parent2.params[key]) / 2\n                    # If both parents had integer values, round to int\n                    if isinstance(parent1.params[key], int) and isinstance(parent2.params[key], int):\n                        blended = int(round(blended))\n                    child_params[key] = blended\n\n        child_model = create_model(parent1.model_type, input_dim=self.input_dim, **child_params)\n        return Individual(\n            model=child_model,\n            model_type=parent1.model_type,\n            params=child_params\n        )\n\n    def evolve(self):\n        \"\"\"Create next generation using selection, crossover, mutation.\"\"\"\n        self.generation += 1\n\n        # Sort by fitness (descending)\n        self.individuals.sort(key=lambda ind: ind.fitness, reverse=True)\n\n        # Elitism: keep best individuals\n        elites = self.individuals[:self.elitism_count]\n\n        # Generate offspring\n        offspring = []\n        while len(offspring) < self.population_size - self.elitism_count:\n            parent1, parent2 = self._select_parents()\n            child = self._crossover(parent1, parent2)\n            child = self._mutate(child)\n            offspring.append(child)\n\n        self.individuals = elites + offspring\n\n        # Ensure we have exactly population_size\n        self.individuals = self.individuals[:self.population_size]\n\n    def get_best(self, n: int = 1) -> List[Individual]:\n        \"\"\"Get top n individuals.\"\"\"\n        sorted_inds = sorted(self.individuals, key=lambda ind: ind.fitness, reverse=True)\n        return sorted_inds[:n]\n\n    def get_diverse_best(self, n: int = 5) -> List[Individual]:\n        \"\"\"Get best individuals ensuring model type diversity.\"\"\"\n        sorted_inds = sorted(self.individuals, key=lambda ind: ind.fitness, reverse=True)\n        selected = []\n        types_seen = set()\n\n        for ind in sorted_inds:\n            if len(selected) >= n:\n                break\n            if ind.model_type not in types_seen or len(selected) < 2:\n                selected.append(ind)\n                types_seen.add(ind.model_type)\n\n        return selected\n","numLines":252,"startLine":1,"totalLines":252}}}
