{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_b681203f1f914f10b499b21c","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"\"\"\n     2→Consensus gating: combine multiple model predictions into final trading signal.\n     3→\"\"\"\n     4→\n     5→import pandas as pd\n     6→import numpy as np\n     7→from typing import List, Dict\n     8→from ..models.population import Individual, Population\n     9→from ..config import ConsensusConfig\n    10→\n    11→\n    12→class ConsensusGate:\n    13→    \"\"\"\n    14→    Gating mechanism that combines predictions from multiple models.\n    15→\n    16→    Requirements:\n    17→    - At least MIN_MODELS_AGREE models must agree on direction\n    18→    - Average confidence must exceed MIN_CONFIDENCE\n    19→    \"\"\"\n    20→\n    21→    def __init__(\n    22→        self,\n    23→        min_models_agree: int = ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n    24→        min_confidence: float = ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n    25→    ):\n    26→        self.min_models_agree = min_models_agree\n    27→        self.min_confidence = min_confidence\n    28→\n    29→    def decide(\n    30→        self,\n    31→        models: List[Individual],\n    32→        X: pd.DataFrame\n    33→    ) -> pd.Series:\n    34→        \"\"\"\n    35→        Generate final trading signals (UP=1, DOWN=0, NO_TRADE=-1).\n    36→\n    37→        Args:\n    38→            models: list of trained model individuals\n    39→            X: feature DataFrame to predict on\n    40→\n    41→        Returns:\n    42→            Series of signals (1, 0, or -1) aligned to X.index\n    43→        \"\"\"\n    44→        signals = pd.Series(index=X.index, data=0)\n    45→        probas_matrix = []\n    46→\n    47→        for ind in models:\n    48→            try:\n    49→                proba = ind.model.predict_proba(X)\n    50→                probas_matrix.append(proba)\n    51→            except Exception as e:\n    52→                continue\n    53→\n    54→        if not probas_matrix:\n    55→            # No model worked, all NO_TRADE\n    56→            signals[:] = -1\n    57→            return signals\n    58→\n    59→        probas_array = np.array(probas_matrix)  # shape: (n_models, n_samples)\n    60→\n    61→        # Average confidence for each sample\n    62→        avg_proba = probas_array.mean(axis=0)\n    63→\n    64→        # Count models predicting UP\n    65→        up_votes = (probas_array >= 0.5).sum(axis=0)\n    66→\n    67→        down_votes = len(probas_matrix) - up_votes\n    68→\n    69→        # Apply gating rules\n    70→        for i, (avg_p, up, down) in enumerate(zip(avg_proba, up_votes, down_votes)):\n    71→            # Check agreement\n    72→            if up >= self.min_models_agree:\n    73→                direction = 1\n    74→                confidence = avg_p\n    75→            elif down >= self.min_models_agree:\n    76→                direction = 0\n    77→                confidence = 1 - avg_p  # confidence in DOWN is 1 - avg_proba\n    78→            else:\n    79→                signals.iloc[i] = -1\n    80→                continue\n    81→\n    82→            # Check confidence threshold\n    83→            if confidence >= self.min_confidence:\n    84→                signals.iloc[i] = direction\n    85→            else:\n    86→                signals.iloc[i] = -1\n    87→\n    88→        return signals\n    89→\n    90→    def adjust_thresholds(\n    91→        self,\n    92→        recent_performance: Dict[str, float],\n    93→        target_accuracy: float = 0.65,\n    94→        target_bets: float = 90.0\n    95→    ):\n    96→        \"\"\"\n    97→        Dynamically adjust consensus thresholds based on recent performance.\n    98→\n    99→        If accuracy is high but bet count too low: lower min_models_agree or min_confidence\n   100→        If accuracy is low: raise thresholds\n   101→        \"\"\"\n   102→        accuracy = recent_performance.get(\"accuracy\", 0.5)\n   103→        bets_per_month = recent_performance.get(\"bets_per_month\", 0.0)\n   104→\n   105→        if accuracy >= target_accuracy and bets_per_month < target_bets:\n   106→            # Too strict, loosen\n   107→            self.min_models_agree = max(1, self.min_models_agree - 1)\n   108→            self.min_confidence = max(0.5, self.min_confidence - 0.02)\n   109→        elif accuracy < target_accuracy:\n   110→            # Too loose, tighten\n   111→            self.min_models_agree = min(5, self.min_models_agree + 1)\n   112→            self.min_confidence = min(0.8, self.min_confidence + 0.02)\n   113→\n   114→        # Clamp to reasonable ranges\n   115→        self.min_models_agree = max(1, min(5, self.min_models_agree))\n   116→        self.min_confidence = max(0.5, min(0.9, self.min_confidence))\n   117→\n   118→    def get_params(self) -> dict:\n   119→        return {\n   120→            \"min_models_agree\": self.min_models_agree,\n   121→            \"min_confidence\": self.min_confidence\n   122→        }\n   123→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8efd0735-0510-4ec3-8516-ffc91c83b918","uuid":"504781ad-7cd8-4869-9750-18cf4a5982e4","timestamp":"2026-03-18T08:28:41.109Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/julien/projects/Bittensor/Arbos/arbos_trading/ensemble/consensus.py","content":"\"\"\"\nConsensus gating: combine multiple model predictions into final trading signal.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\nfrom typing import List, Dict\nfrom ..models.population import Individual, Population\nfrom ..config import ConsensusConfig\n\n\nclass ConsensusGate:\n    \"\"\"\n    Gating mechanism that combines predictions from multiple models.\n\n    Requirements:\n    - At least MIN_MODELS_AGREE models must agree on direction\n    - Average confidence must exceed MIN_CONFIDENCE\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        min_models_agree: int = ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n        min_confidence: float = ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n    ):\n        self.min_models_agree = min_models_agree\n        self.min_confidence = min_confidence\n\n    def decide(\n        self,\n        models: List[Individual],\n        X: pd.DataFrame\n    ) -> pd.Series:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate final trading signals (UP=1, DOWN=0, NO_TRADE=-1).\n\n        Args:\n            models: list of trained model individuals\n            X: feature DataFrame to predict on\n\n        Returns:\n            Series of signals (1, 0, or -1) aligned to X.index\n        \"\"\"\n        signals = pd.Series(index=X.index, data=0)\n        probas_matrix = []\n\n        for ind in models:\n            try:\n                proba = ind.model.predict_proba(X)\n                probas_matrix.append(proba)\n            except Exception as e:\n                continue\n\n        if not probas_matrix:\n            # No model worked, all NO_TRADE\n            signals[:] = -1\n            return signals\n\n        probas_array = np.array(probas_matrix)  # shape: (n_models, n_samples)\n\n        # Average confidence for each sample\n        avg_proba = probas_array.mean(axis=0)\n\n        # Count models predicting UP\n        up_votes = (probas_array >= 0.5).sum(axis=0)\n\n        down_votes = len(probas_matrix) - up_votes\n\n        # Apply gating rules\n        for i, (avg_p, up, down) in enumerate(zip(avg_proba, up_votes, down_votes)):\n            # Check agreement\n            if up >= self.min_models_agree:\n                direction = 1\n                confidence = avg_p\n            elif down >= self.min_models_agree:\n                direction = 0\n                confidence = 1 - avg_p  # confidence in DOWN is 1 - avg_proba\n            else:\n                signals.iloc[i] = -1\n                continue\n\n            # Check confidence threshold\n            if confidence >= self.min_confidence:\n                signals.iloc[i] = direction\n            else:\n                signals.iloc[i] = -1\n\n        return signals\n\n    def adjust_thresholds(\n        self,\n        recent_performance: Dict[str, float],\n        target_accuracy: float = 0.65,\n        target_bets: float = 90.0\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Dynamically adjust consensus thresholds based on recent performance.\n\n        If accuracy is high but bet count too low: lower min_models_agree or min_confidence\n        If accuracy is low: raise thresholds\n        \"\"\"\n        accuracy = recent_performance.get(\"accuracy\", 0.5)\n        bets_per_month = recent_performance.get(\"bets_per_month\", 0.0)\n\n        if accuracy >= target_accuracy and bets_per_month < target_bets:\n            # Too strict, loosen\n            self.min_models_agree = max(1, self.min_models_agree - 1)\n            self.min_confidence = max(0.5, self.min_confidence - 0.02)\n        elif accuracy < target_accuracy:\n            # Too loose, tighten\n            self.min_models_agree = min(5, self.min_models_agree + 1)\n            self.min_confidence = min(0.8, self.min_confidence + 0.02)\n\n        # Clamp to reasonable ranges\n        self.min_models_agree = max(1, min(5, self.min_models_agree))\n        self.min_confidence = max(0.5, min(0.9, self.min_confidence))\n\n    def get_params(self) -> dict:\n        return {\n            \"min_models_agree\": self.min_models_agree,\n            \"min_confidence\": self.min_confidence\n        }\n","numLines":123,"startLine":1,"totalLines":123}}}

Combined transaction cost: fee + slippage\n        entry_cost = self.fee_rate + self.slippage\n        exit_cost = self.fee_rate + self.slippage\n\n        # Align signals and prices\n        signals = signals.reindex(prices.index).fillna(-1)\n\n        # Build equity curve indexed by signals.index\n        equity = pd.Series(index=signals.index, dtype=float)\n        equity.iloc[0] = capital\n\n        # Iterate through each timestamp, use signal to open a trade that closes at next timestamp\n        for i in range(len(signals) - 1):\n            sig = signals.iloc[i]\n            current_price = prices.iloc[i]\n            next_price = prices.iloc[i+1]\n\n            if sig == -1:\n                # No trade, carry forward capital\n                equity.iloc[i+1] = capital\n                continue\n\n            if sig == 1:  # long\n                entry = current_price * (1 + entry_cost)\n                exit = next_price * (1 - exit_cost)\n                ret = (exit / entry) - 1\n            else:  # sig == 0, short\n                entry = current_price * (1 - entry_cost)\n                exit = next_price * (1 + exit_cost)\n                ret = (entry / exit) - 1\n\n            # Apply bet size\n            capital *= (1 + ret * self.bet_size)\n\n            self.trades.append({\n                \"entry_time\": signals.index[i],\n                \"exit_time\": signals.index[i+1],\n                \"entry_price\": entry,\n                \"exit_price\": exit,\n                \"return\": ret,\n                \"pnl\": capital - self.initial_capital\n            })\n\n            equity.iloc[i+1] = capital\n\n        # Fill any remaining NaNs in equity with last value\n        equity = equity.ffill()\n\n        self.equity_curve = equity\n\n        # Calculate metrics\n        self.metrics = self._compute_metrics()\n\n        return self.metrics\n\n    def _compute_metrics(self) -> Dict[str, float]:\n        \"\"\"Compute metrics from simulation.\"\"\"\n        if not self.trades:\n            return {\n                \"accuracy\": 0.0,\n                \"bets_per_month\": 0.0,\n                \"sharpe\": 0.0,\n                \"max_drawdown\": 0.0,\n                \"total_return\": 0.0,\n                \"n_trades\": 0,\n                \"profit_factor\": 0.0,\n                \"final_equity\": self.initial_capital\n            }\n\n        returns = [t[\"return\"] for t in self.trades]\n        winning = sum(1 for r in returns if r > 0)\n        accuracy = winning / len(returns)\n\n        # Bets per month (entries)\n        n_entries = len(self.trades)\n        if self.equity_curve is not None and len(self.equity_curve) > 0:\n            period_days = len(self.equity_curve) / (24 * 4)  # 15-min candles to days\n        else:\n            period_days = 0\n        months = period_days / 30.44\n        bets_per_month = n_entries / months if months > 0 else 0\n\n        # Sharpe\n        if self.equity_curve is not None:\n            eq_returns = self.equity_curve.pct_change().dropna()\n            if eq_returns.std() > 0:\n                sharpe = (eq_returns.mean() / eq_returns.std()) * np.sqrt(365 * 24 * 4)\n            else:\n                sharpe = 0.0\n\n            # Max drawdown\n            running_max = self.equity_curve.expanding().max()\n            drawdown = (self.equity_curve - running_max) / running_max\n            max_drawdown = abs(drawdown.min())\n        else:\n            sharpe = 0.0\n            max_drawdown = 0.0\n\n        total_return = (self.equity_curve.iloc[-1] - self.initial_capital) / self.initial_capital\n\n        gross_profit = sum(r for r in returns if r > 0)\n        gross_loss = abs(sum(r for r in returns if r < 0))\n        profit_factor = gross_profit / (gross_loss + 1e-10)\n\n        return {\n            \"accuracy\": accuracy,\n            \"bets_per_month\": bets_per_month,\n            \"sharpe\": sharpe,\n            \"max_drawdown\": max_drawdown,\n            \"total_return\": total_return,\n            \"n_trades\": len(self.trades),\n            \"profit_factor\": profit_factor,\n            \"final_equity\": self.equity_curve.iloc[-1] if self.equity_curve is not None else self.initial_capital,\n            \"gross_profit\": gross_profit,\n            \"gross_loss\": gross_loss\n        }\n\n    def get_equity_curve(self) -> pd.Series:\n        return self.equity_curve\n\n    def get_trades(self) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        return pd.DataFrame(self.trades)\n","numLines":174,"startLine":1,"totalLines":174}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_ffc09d0d988b4c02a574808a","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"\"\"\n     2→Data processing and cleaning utilities.\n     3→\"\"\"\n     4→\n     5→import pandas as pd\n     6→import numpy as np\n     7→from typing import Tuple\n     8→\n     9→\n    10→def clean_data(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    11→    \"\"\"\n    12→    Clean data: remove duplicates, handle missing values, ensure proper sorting.\n    13→    \"\"\"\n    14→    df = df.copy()\n    15→\n    16→    # Ensure sorted by time\n    17→    df = df.sort_values(\"open_time\").reset_index(drop=True)\n    18→\n    19→    # Remove duplicates\n    20→    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=[\"open_time\"])\n    21→\n    22→    # Check for gaps in 15-minute intervals\n    23→    expected_diff = pd.Timedelta(minutes=15)\n    24→    time_diffs = df[\"open_time\"].diff()\n    25→    gaps = time_diffs > expected_diff\n    26→\n    27→    if gaps.any():\n    28→        print(f\"Warning: Found {gaps.sum()} gaps in data.\")\n    29→\n    30→    # Forward fill missing OHLC if any (but not volume)\n    31→    for col in [\"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\"]:\n    32→        df[col] = df[col].ffill()\n    33→\n    34→    # Drop rows where close is still NaN\n    35→    df = df.dropna(subset=[\"close\"])\n    36→\n    37→    return df\n    38→\n    39→\n    40→def resample_to_horizon(\n    41→    df: pd.DataFrame,\n    42→    horizon_minutes: int,\n    43→    factor: int  # e.g., 4 for 1h (4 * 15m)\n    44→) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    45→    \"\"\"\n    46→    Resample 15-minute data to a longer horizon by aggregating.\n    47→    For horizons > 15m, we aggregate 'factor' number of 15m candles.\n    48→    \"\"\"\n    49→    if factor <= 1:\n    50→        return df.copy()\n    51→\n    52→    # Set index to open_time for resampling\n    53→    df_idx = df.set_index(\"open_time\")\n    54→\n    55→    # OHLC: open=first, high=max, low=min, close=last, volume=sum\n    56→    resampled = df_idx[[\"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\"]].resample(f\"{horizon_minutes}T\").agg({\n    57→        \"open\": \"first\",\n    58→        \"high\": \"max\",\n    59→        \"low\": \"min\",\n    60→        \"close\": \"last\",\n    61→        \"volume\": \"sum\"\n    62→    })\n    63→\n    64→    resampled = resampled.dropna().reset_index()\n    65→\n    66→    return resampled\n    67→\n    68→\n    69→def create_target(df: pd.DataFrame, forward_periods: int = 1) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    70→    \"\"\"\n    71→    Create binary target: 1 if price goes UP in next 15-minute candle, 0 if DOWN.\n    72→    \"\"\"\n    73→    df = df.copy()\n    74→\n    75→    # Future close price (next 15-minute candle)\n    76→    df[\"future_close\"] = df[\"close\"].shift(-forward_periods)\n    77→\n    78→    # Binary direction: 1 if close > current close, else 0\n    79→    df[\"target\"] = (df[\"future_close\"] > df[\"close\"]).astype(int)\n    80→\n    81→    return df\n    82→\n    83→\n    84→def calculate_returns(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    85→    \"\"\"Calculate various return metrics.\"\"\"\n    86→    df = df.copy()\n    87→\n    88→    # Ensure sorted by time\n    df = df.sort_values(\"open_time\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n    # Remove duplicates\n    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=[\"open_time\"])\n\n    # Check for gaps in 15-minute intervals\n    expected_diff = pd.Timedelta(minutes=15)\n    time_diffs = df[\"open_time\"].diff()\n    gaps = time_diffs > expected_diff\n\n    if gaps.any():\n        print(f\"Warning: Found {gaps.sum()} gaps in data.\")\n\n    # Forward fill missing OHLC if any (but not volume)\n    for col in [\"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\"]:\n        df[col] = df[col].ffill()\n\n    # Drop rows where close is still NaN\n    df = df.dropna(subset=[\"close\"])\n\n    return df\n\n\ndef resample_to_horizon(\n    df: pd.DataFrame,\n    horizon_minutes: int,\n    factor: int  # e.g., 4 for 1h (4 * 15m)\n) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    \"\"\"\n    Resample 15-minute data to a longer horizon by aggregating.\n    For horizons > 15m, we aggregate 'factor' number of 15m candles.\n    \"\"\"\n    if factor <= 1:\n        return df.copy()\n\n    # Set index to open_time for resampling\n    df_idx = df.set_index(\"open_time\")\n\n    # OHLC: open=first, high=max, low=min, close=last, volume=sum\n    resampled = df_idx[[\"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\"]].resample(f\"{horizon_minutes}T\").agg({\n        \"open\": \"first\",\n        \"high\": \"max\",\n        \"low\": \"min\",\n        \"close\": \"last\",\n        \"volume\": \"sum\"\n    })\n\n    resampled = resampled.dropna().reset_index()\n\n    return resampled\n\n\ndef create_target(df: pd.DataFrame, forward_periods: int = 1) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    \"\"\"\n    Create binary target: 1 if price goes UP in next 15-minute candle, 0 if DOWN.\n    \"\"\"\n    df = df.copy()\n\n    # Future close price (next 15-minute candle)\n    df[\"future_close\"] = df[\"close\"].shift(-forward_periods)\n\n    # Binary direction: 1 if close > current close, else 0\n    df[\"target\"] = (df[\"future_close\"] > df[\"close\"]).astype(int)\n\n    return df\n\n\ndef calculate_returns(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    \"\"\"Calculate various return metrics.\"\"\"\n    df = df.copy()\n\n    # horizon -> population\n        self.main_features = []  # list of feature names produced by this ensemble\n        self.is_fitted = False\n\n    def fit_horizon_models(\n        self,\n        horizon_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame],\n        target_series: pd.Series\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Train a separate model population for each horizon.\n\n        Args:\n            horizon_data: dict of {horizon: DataFrame} with features for that horizon\n            target_series: target series (same for all, aligned to base timeframe)\n        \"\"\"\n        self.horizon_models = {}\n\n        horizons = list(horizon_data.items())\n        print(f\"Fitting horizon ensembles for {len(horizons)} horizons\", flush=True)\n        for idx, (horizon, df) in enumerate(horizons):\n            if df.empty:\n                print(f\"  Horizon {horizon}: empty, skipping\", flush=True)\n                continue\n\n            print(f\"  Horizon {horizon} ({idx+1}/{len(horizons)}): processing {len(df)} rows\", flush=True)\n            # Align target to this horizon's timestamps\n            df_indexed = df.set_index(\"open_time\")\n            target_aligned = target_series.reindex(df_indexed.index)\n\n            # Prepare features (exclude time and target columns)\n            feature_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c not in [\"open_time\", \"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\"]]\n            X = df[feature_cols]\n            print(f\"    Feature count: {len(feature_cols)}\", flush=True)\n\n            # Create population (use only random_forest for speed and stability)\n            pop = Population(\n                population_size=5,  # small for horizon models\n                model_types=[\"random_forest\"],\n                elitism_count=1\n            )\n            pop.initialize(input_dim=len(feature_cols), feature_names=feature_cols)\n\n            # Quick training: just fit all models (simplified, should be evolutionary)\n            print(f\"    Training {len(pop.individuals)} models...\", flush=True)\n            self._train_population(pop, X, target_aligned)\n            self.horizon_models[horizon] = pop\n            print(f\"    Completed horizon {horizon}\", flush=True)\n\n        self.is_fitted = True\n        print(\"All horizon ensembles fitted\", flush=True)\n\n    def _train_population(self, population: Population, X: pd.DataFrame, y: pd.Series):\n        \"\"\"Fit all models in a population.\"\"\"\n        total = len(population.individuals)\n        for i, ind in enumerate(population.individuals):\n            try:\n                print(f\"[horizon]   Training model {i+1}/{total} ({ind.model_type})...\", flush=True)\n                ind.model.fit(X, y)\n                print(f\"[horizon]   Model {i+1} trained\", flush=True)\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"[horizon]   Model {i+1} failed: {e}\", flush=True)\n                # Set very low fitness if model fails\n                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n    def generate_features(\n        self,\n        horizon_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame],\n        base_index: pd.DatetimeIndex\n    ) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate horizon ensemble features: predictions from each horizon's best model.\n\n        Returns:\n            DataFrame with columns like: h_1h_pred, h_1h_proba, h_4h_pred, ...\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self.is_fitted:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"HorizonEnsemble must be fitted first\")\n\n        features = pd.DataFrame(index=base_index)\n\n        for horizon, pop in self.horizon_models.items():\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n\n            # Forward fill any missing features (from resampling gaps)\n            X_h_features = X_h_features.ffill()\n\n            # Generate predictions\n            try:\n                proba = best.model.predict_proba(X_h_features)\n                pred = best.model.predict(X_h_features, threshold=0.5)\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_proba\"] = proba\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_pred\"] = pred\n            except Exception as e:\n                # If prediction fails, fill with 0.5 (neutral)\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_proba\"] = 0.5\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_pred\"] = 0\n\n        self.main_features = list(features.columns)\n        return features\n\n    def get_feature_names(self) -> List[str]:\n        return self.main_features\n","numLines":133,"startLine":1,"totalLines":133}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"gen-1773822596-oJEwc7UkI1grLQNVfcwT","type":"message","role":"assistant","container":null,"content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"I see: In fit_horizon_models, they align the target by reindexing: `target_aligned = target_series.reindex(df_indexed.index)`. This is the target (y) that comes from the base timeframe target series (which is aligned to 15m timestamps). Then they train horizon models on horizon data to predict this target. However, the horizon data is at a coarser frequency (e.g., 1h) but the target is originally 15m. When you reindex the 15m target to 1h timestamps, you get NaNs because 1h timestamps are sparser and may not match exactly. That's why \"Input y contains NaN.\" The horizon models are trying to train on NaN targets. That's a bug.\n\nWe need to align the target properly. For horizon models, the target should be the same forward prediction but on the horizon timeframe. Ideally, for a 1h horizon, you want to predict the direction over the next 1h (4 x 15m). But the code tries to use the 15m target reindexed, which doesn't work.\n\nThis issue existed before; maybe it was never functional. But in Step 18, with only 15m horizon, it didn't try to train horizon models. In Step 19, we added horizons, so it attempts to train them and fails. However, the base model runs and produces results (with 93 features, including horizon features that were forward-filled). So the base model is using horizon features that are just technical indicators from resampled data, not predictions. That's okay as features, but the horizon ensemble training is broken. It doesn't affect the base model training because horizon ensemble is used to generate additional features? Actually in `prepare_features`, they call `generate_horizon_features` which produces horizon-derived features (technical indicators from resampled data). Target: >=65% accuracy, >90 bets/month, positive Sharpe.\n\n## Current Status: 📊 Step 19 Completed – High Accuracy, Negative Sharpe\n\n### Step 19 Configuration\n- **Population:** POPULATION_SIZE=3, MODEL_TYPES=[\"random_forest\"]\n- **RF hyperparameter variation:**\n  - n_estimators: [30, 50]\n  - max_depth: [6, 8]\n  - min_samples_leaf: [10, 15]\n- **Training data:** ONLINE_DATA_DAYS=90, MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES=3000\n- **Validation:** TRAIN_DAYS=14, VALID_DAYS=7, MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS=30\n- **Horizons:** {\"15m\":1, \"1h\":4, \"4h\":16} (multi-horizon technical features)\n- **Consensus:** MIN_MODELS_AGREE=2, MIN_CONFIDENCE=0.65\n\n### Step 19 Results\nRun: `results/20260318_082918`\n\n- **Validation accuracy:** 0.8426 (exceeds 65% target)\n- **Bets/month:** 469.65 (far exceeds 90 target)\n- **Sharpe:** -30.59 (deeply negative)\n- **Total return:** -2.26%\n- **Max drawdown:** 2.29%\n- **Profit factor:** 0.331 (gross profit 0.0565, gross loss 0.1707)\n- **Number of trades (validation):** 108\n- **Latest signal:** -1 (NO_TRADE)\n- **Population fitness:** best ~0.81 (training accuracy)\n\n### Step 19 Observations\n- **Paradox:** Accuracy is excellent (84%) but profitability is terrible (negative expectancy).\n- Trade distribution: 108 trades → ~91 wins (positive returns) and ~17 losses. However, average win ~0.062% while average loss ~1.0%. Losses are ~16× larger than wins → negative expectancy despite high win rate.\n- The simulator produced negative total return and very low profit factor (0.33), confirming that the model's correct predictions capture only tiny moves while incorrect predictions result in large adverse moves.\n- Horizon ensemble training failed with \"Input y contains NaN.\" errors due to misaligned target series. Horizon features (resampled technicals) are used by the base model, but horizon model predictions are not integrated yet.\n- Consensus gate with confidence=0.65 allowed many trades (469/mo), so frequency is not an issue.\n- Training fitness (~0.81) is high but validation Sharpe extremely negative, indicating overfitting to training regime or regime shift between training and validation.\n- Runtime ~1-2 min, acceptable.\n\n### Diagnosis – Why High Accuracy ≠ Profitability?\n1. **Asymmetric win/loss distribution:** Model predicts small bullish/bearish moves correctly most of the time, but when wrong, the market moves strongly against the position (e.g., strong one-sided moves). Without stops, a single large loss can wipe out many small gains.\n2. **Volatility-blind position sizing:** Fixed bet size (20% of capital) exposed to large losses during high volatility or gap moves.\n3. **No stop-loss:** The simulator holds for exactly one 15-minute period; if price moves sharply against within that period, the full loss is taken.\n4. **Potential overfitting:** Validation Sharpe suggests the trained models perform poorly OOS despite training accuracy, possibly due to regime change or data snooping.\n5. **Horizon ensemble integration broken:** The horizon model predictions are not used, missing potential cross-horizon confirmation that could improve risk-adjusted returns.\n6. **Target definition:** Predicting any directional move regardless of magnitude may lead to taking low-edge trades with tiny expected gains but occasional large losses.\n\n### Step 20 Plan – Risk-Aware Trading & Horizon Fix\n\nWe need to convert high accuracy into positive expectancy by managing risk and improving model quality.\n\n#### A. Fix Horizon Ensemble Training\n- Bug: `HorizonEnsemble.fit_horizon_models()` reindexes base target (15m) to horizon timestamps, causing NaNs. Should use the horizon-specific `target` column directly from the horizon DataFrame.\n- Modification: In `fit_horizon_models`, for each horizon, use `df['target']` after dropping NaNs, instead of reindexing external target_series.\n- This enables horizon models to be trained properly. Later we can consider using their predictions as features or in consensus.\n\n#### B. Introduce Volatility-Aware Position Sizing & Stop-Loss\n- Modify `SimulatedTrader` to support:\n  - Dynamic bet size based on recent volatility (e.g., ATR normalized) to reduce exposure during high volatility.\n  - Optional stop-loss: exit early if price moves against entry by X% (e.g., 1× ATR) before the fixed holding period.\n  - Alternatively, use a fixed fractional position sizing with a maximum per-trade risk cap.\n- Initially simpler: reduce `POSITION_SIZE` from 0.2 to 0.05 (5% of capital per trade) to limit drawdown. This alone can improve Sharpe if the model's edge is real but losses are amplified by sizing.\n- But better: compute position size as `min(bet_size, risk_capital / (entry_price * ATR))` for a risk-based approach.\n\n#### C. Refine Consensus Thresholds Based on Recent Performance\n- The `ConsensusGate.adjust_thresholds()` method exists but is not called automatically. Add a step after walk-forward to call `adjust_thresholds(metrics)` to adapt thresholds for the next iteration.\n- This helps tune between frequency and accuracy.\n\n#### D. Possibly Increase Diversity\n- If runtime allows, increase POPULATION_SIZE to 5 and extend hyperparameter ranges (e.g., max_depth up to 10, n_estimators up to 100) to search for more robust models.\n- But memory constraints previously caused OOM; monitor usage.\n\n#### E. Fitness Improvement\n- The population fitness already uses Sharpe during validation rescoring. That's good. We'll also log profit_factor and penalize low profit_factor.\n\n#### Step 20 Proposed Configuration (tentative)\n- Keep: POPULATION_SIZE=3, MODEL_TYPES=[\"random_forest\"], ONLINE_DATA_DAYS=90, MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES=3000\n- Expand RF: max_depth ∈ [8, 10, 12], n_estimators ∈ [50, 100]\n- Validation: TRAIN_DAYS=14, VALID_DAYS=7 (keep)\n- Consensus: start with MIN_CONFIDENCE=0.65, MIN_MODELS_AGREE=2; call `adjust_thresholds()` based on previous metrics.\n- Position sizing: reduce POSITION_SIZE to 0.05 or implement ATR-based sizing.\n- Horizon: keep 1h and 4h; fix training bug.\n- Optionally add stop-loss (e.g., 2× ATR) in simulator.\n\n#### Targets\n- Accuracy ≥ 0.65\n- Bets/month ≥ 90\n- Sharpe > 1.0 (positive)\n- Profit factor > 1.2\n\nWe will implement these changes incrementally, starting with fixing horizon training and reducing position size to test if Sharpe improves.\n\n## Previous Critical Steps (for context)\n- **Step 14:** conf=0.65, depth=8, min_leaf=10, pop=5, max_train=4000 → accuracy 0.5517, bets 126.11/mo, Sharpe -27.37.\n- **Steps 15 & 16:** OOM kills.\n- **Step 17:** Timeout after 68m.\n- **Step 18:** Excessive confidence → zero trades."}}],"model":"stepfun/step-3.5-flash:free","stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":null,"cache_read_input_tokens":null,"cache_creation":null,"inference_geo":null,"server_tool_use":null,"service_tier":null},"provider":"StepFun","context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8efd0735-0510-4ec3-8516-ffc91c83b918","uuid":"8ca29171-095b-4146-898d-fb46ff5c7208"}

Horizon features (resampled technicals) are used by the base model, but horizon model predictions are not integrated yet.\n- Consensus gate with confidence=0.65 allowed many trades (469/mo), so frequency is not an issue.\n- Training fitness (~0.81) is high but validation Sharpe extremely negative, indicating overfitting to training regime or regime shift between training and validation.\n- Runtime ~1-2 min, acceptable.\n\n### Diagnosis – Why High Accuracy ≠ Profitability?\n1. **Asymmetric win/loss distribution:** Model predicts small bullish/bearish moves correctly most of the time, but when wrong, the market moves strongly against the position (e.g., strong one-sided moves). Without stops, a single large loss can wipe out many small gains.\n2. **Volatility-blind position sizing:** Fixed bet size (20% of capital) exposed to large losses during high volatility or gap moves.\n3. **No stop-loss:** The simulator holds for exactly one 15-minute period; if price moves sharply against within that period, the full loss is taken.\n4. **Potential overfitting:** Validation Sharpe suggests the trained models perform poorly OOS despite training accuracy, possibly due to regime change or data snooping.\n5. **Horizon ensemble integration broken:** The horizon model predictions are not used, missing potential cross-horizon confirmation that could improve risk-adjusted returns.\n6. **Target definition:** Predicting any directional move regardless of magnitude may lead to taking low-edge trades with tiny expected gains but occasional large losses.\n\n### Step 20 Plan – Risk-Aware Trading & Horizon Fix\n\nWe need to convert high accuracy into positive expectancy by managing risk and improving model quality.\n\n#### A. This alone can improve Sharpe if the model's edge is real but losses are amplified by sizing.\n- But better: compute position size as `min(bet_size, risk_capital / (entry_price * ATR))` for a risk-based approach.\n\n#### C. Refine Consensus Thresholds Based on Recent Performance\n- The `ConsensusGate.adjust_thresholds()` method exists but is not called automatically. Build equity curve indexed by signals.index\n        equity = pd.Series(index=signals.index, dtype=float)\n        equity.iloc[0] = capital\n\n        # Iterate through each timestamp, use signal to open a trade that closes at next timestamp\n        for i in range(len(signals) - 1):\n            sig = signals.iloc[i]\n            current_price = prices.iloc[i]\n            next_price = prices.iloc[i+1]\n\n            if sig == -1:\n                # Combined transaction cost: fee + slippage\n        entry_cost = self.fee_rate + self.slippage\n        exit_cost = self.fee_rate + self.slippage\n\n        # Determine if we have high/low for stop-loss\n        use_stop = self.stop_loss_pct > 0\n        if isinstance(prices, pd.DataFrame):\n            # Expect columns: 'close', optionally 'high', 'low'\n            if 'high' in prices.columns and 'low' in prices.columns:\n                close_series = prices['close']\n                high_series = prices['high']\n                low_series = prices['low']\n            else:\n                close_series = prices['close']\n                high_series = low_series = close_series\n        else:\n            close_series = prices\n            high_series = low_series = prices\n\n        # Align signals and prices\n        signals = signals.reindex(close_series.index).fillna(-1)\n\n        # Build equity curve indexed by signals.index\n        equity = pd.Series(index=signals.index, dtype=float)\n        equity.iloc[0] = capital\n\n        # Iterate through each timestamp, use signal to open a trade that closes at next timestamp\n        for i in range(len(signals) - 1):\n            sig = signals.iloc[i]\n            current_price = close_series.iloc[i]\n            next_close = close_series.iloc[i+1]\n            next_high = high_series.iloc[i+1]\n            next_low = low_series.iloc[i+1]\n\n            if sig == -1:\n                # No trade, carry forward capital\n                equity.iloc[i+1] = capital\n                continue\n\n            # Determine entry price with cost\n            if sig == 1:  # long\n                entry = current_price * (1 + entry_cost)\n                # Determine exit price considering stop-loss\n                stop_price = entry * (1 - self.stop_loss_pct) if use_stop else None\n                exit_price = next_close * (1 - exit_cost)\n                if use_stop and next_low <= stop_price:\n                    # Stop triggered: exit at stop price (with exit cost)\n                    exit_price = stop_price * (1 - exit_cost)\n                ret = (exit_price / entry) - 1\n            else:  # short\n                entry = current_price * (1 - entry_cost)\n                stop_price = entry * (1 + self.stop_loss_pct) if use_stop else None\n                exit_price = next_close * (1 + exit_cost)\n                if use_stop and next_high >= stop_price:\n                    exit_price = stop_price * (1 + exit_cost)\n                ret = (entry / exit_price) - 1\n\n            # Apply bet size\n            capital *= (1 + ret * self.bet_size)\n\n            self.trades.append({\n                \"entry_time\": signals.index[i],\n                \"exit_time\": signals.index[i+1],\n                \"entry_price\": entry,\n                \"exit_price\": exit_price,\n                \"return\": ret,\n                \"pnl\": capital - self.initial_capital,\n                \"stopped\": use_stop and ((sig == 1 and next_low <= stop_price) or (sig == 0 and next_high >= stop_price))\n            })\n\n            equity.iloc[i+1] = capital\n\n        # Fill any remaining NaNs in equity with last value\n        equity = equity.ffill()\n\n        self.equity_curve = equity\n\n        # Calculate metrics\n        self.metrics = self._compute_metrics()\n\n        return self.metrics"}}],"model":"stepfun/step-3.5-flash:free","stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":null,"cache_read_input_tokens":null,"cache_creation":null,"inference_geo":null,"server_tool_use":null,"service_tier":null},"provider":"StepFun","context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8efd0735-0510-4ec3-8516-ffc91c83b918","uuid":"52575a6c-16d6-421e-84d2-9b0fe05a2ae2"}

prices['high']\n                low_series = prices['low']\n            else:\n                close_series = prices['close']\n                high_series = low_series = close_series\n        else:\n            close_series = prices\n            high_series = low_series = prices\n\n        # Align signals and prices\n        signals = signals.reindex(close_series.index).fillna(-1)\n\n        # Build equity curve indexed by signals.index\n        equity = pd.Series(index=signals.index, dtype=float)\n        equity.iloc[0] = capital\n\n        # Iterate through each timestamp, use signal to open a trade that closes at next timestamp\n        for i in range(len(signals) - 1):\n            sig = signals.iloc[i]\n            current_price = close_series.iloc[i]\n            next_close = close_series.iloc[i+1]\n            next_high = high_series.iloc[i+1]\n            next_low = low_series.iloc[i+1]\n\n            if sig == -1:\n                # No trade, carry forward capital\n                equity.iloc[i+1] = capital\n                continue\n\n            # Determine entry price with cost\n            if sig == 1:  # long\n                entry = current_price * (1 + entry_cost)\n                # Determine exit price considering stop-loss\n                stop_price = entry * (1 - self.stop_loss_pct) if use_stop else None\n                exit_price = next_close * (1 - exit_cost)\n                if use_stop and next_low <= stop_price:\n                    # Stop triggered: exit at stop price (with exit cost)\n                    exit_price = stop_price * (1 - exit_cost)\n                ret = (exit_price / entry) - 1\n            else:  # short\n                entry = current_price * (1 - entry_cost)\n                stop_price = entry * (1 + self.stop_loss_pct) if use_stop else None\n                exit_price = next_close * (1 + exit_cost)\n                if use_stop and next_high >= stop_price:\n                    exit_price = stop_price * (1 + exit_cost)\n                ret = (entry / exit_price) - 1\n\n            # Apply bet size\n            capital *= (1 + ret * self.bet_size)\n\n            self.trades.append({\n                \"entry_time\": signals.index[i],\n                \"exit_time\": signals.index[i+1],\n                \"entry_price\": entry,\n                \"exit_price\": exit_price,\n                \"return\": ret,\n                \"pnl\": capital - self.initial_capital,\n                \"stopped\": use_stop and ((sig == 1 and next_low <= stop_price) or (sig == 0 and next_high >= stop_price))\n            })\n\n            equity.iloc[i+1] = capital\n\n        # Fill any remaining NaNs in equity with last value\n        equity = equity.ffill()\n\n        self.equity_curve = pd\nimport numpy as np\nfrom typing import Optional, Dict\nfrom .metrics import calculate_metrics\n\n\nclass SimulatedTrader:\n    \"\"\"\n    Simulates paper trading given a sequence of signals and price data.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        initial_capital: float = 10000.0,\n        fee_rate: float = 0.001,\n        slippage: float = 0.0005,\n        bet_size: float = 1.0,\n        stop_loss_pct: float = 0.0\n    ):\n        self.initial_capital = initial_capital\n        self.fee_rate = fee_rate\n        self.slippage = slippage\n        self.bet_size = bet_size\n        self.stop_loss_pct = stop_loss_pct\n\n        self.equity_curve = None\n        self.trades = []\n        self.metrics = {}\n\n    def run(\n        self,\n        signals: pd.Series,\n        prices: pd.Series,\n        entry_price_col: str = \"close\"\n    ) -> Dict[str, float]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Run simulation with fixed 1-period holding.\n\n        Args:\n            signals: Series of signals (1=long, 0=short, -1=no trade)\n            prices: Series of prices (typically close)\n            entry_price_col: ignored\n\n        Returns:\n            dict of performance metrics\n        \"\"\"\n        self.trades = []\n\n        capital = self.initial_capital\n        # Combined transaction cost: fee + slippage\n        entry_cost = self.fee_rate + self.slippage\n        exit_cost = self.fee_rate + self.slippage\n\n        # Align signals and prices\n        signals = signals.reindex(prices.index).fillna(-1)\n\n        # Build equity curve indexed by signals.index\n        equity = pd.Series(index=signals.index, dtype=float)\n        equity.iloc[0] = capital\n\n        # Iterate through each timestamp, use signal to open a trade that closes at next timestamp\n        for i in range(len(signals) - 1):\n            sig = signals.iloc[i]\n            current_price = prices.iloc[i]\n            next_price = prices.iloc[i+1]\n\n            if sig == -1:\n                # No trade, carry forward capital\n                equity.iloc[i+1] = capital\n                continue\n\n            if sig == 1:  # long\n                entry = current_price * (1 + entry_cost)\n                exit = next_price * (1 - exit_cost)\n                ret = (exit / entry) - 1\n            else:  # sig == 0, short\n                entry = current_price * (1 - entry_cost)\n                exit = next_price * (1 + exit_cost)\n                ret = (entry / exit) - 1\n\n            # Apply bet size\n            capital *= (1 + ret * self.bet_size)\n\n            self.trades.append({\n                \"entry_time\": signals.index[i],\n                \"exit_time\": signals.index[i+1],\n                \"entry_price\": entry,\n                \"exit_price\": exit,\n                \"return\": ret,\n                \"pnl\": capital - self.initial_capital\n            })\n\n            equity.iloc[i+1] = capital\n\n        # Fill any remaining NaNs in equity with last value\n        equity = equity.ffill()\n\n        self.equity_curve = equity\n\n        # Calculate metrics\n        self.metrics = self._compute_metrics()\n\n        return self.metrics\n\n    def _compute_metrics(self) -> Dict[str, float]:\n        \"\"\"Compute metrics from simulation.\"\"\"\n        if not self.trades:\n            return {\n                \"accuracy\": 0.0,\n                \"bets_per_month\": 0.0,\n                \"sharpe\": 0.0,\n                \"max_drawdown\": 0.0,\n                \"total_return\": 0.0,\n                \"n_trades\": 0,\n                \"profit_factor\": 0.0,\n                \"final_equity\": self.initial_capital\n            }\n\n        returns = [t[\"return\"] for t in self.trades]\n        winning = sum(1 for r in returns if r > 0)\n        accuracy = winning / len(returns)\n\n        # Bets per month (entries)\n        n_entries = len(self.trades)\n        if self.equity_curve is not None and len(self.equity_curve) > 0:\n            period_days = len(self.equity_curve) / (24 * 4)  # 15-min candles to days\n        else:\n            period_days = 0\n        months = period_days / 30.44\n        bets_per_month = n_entries / months if months > 0 else 0\n\n        # Sharpe\n        if self.equity_curve is not None:\n            eq_returns = self.equity_curve.pct_change().dropna()\n            if eq_returns.std() > 0:\n                sharpe = (eq_returns.mean() / eq_returns.std()) * np.sqrt(365 * 24 * 4)\n            else:\n                sharpe = 0.0\n\n            # Max drawdown\n            running_max = self.equity_curve.expanding().max()\n            drawdown = (self.equity_curve - running_max) / running_max\n            max_drawdown = abs(drawdown.min())\n        else:\n            sharpe = 0.0\n            max_drawdown = 0.0\n\n        total_return = (self.equity_curve.iloc[-1] - self.initial_capital) / self.initial_capital\n\n        gross_profit = sum(r for r in returns if r > 0)\n        gross_loss = abs(sum(r for r in returns if r < 0))\n        profit_factor = gross_profit / (gross_loss + 1e-10)\n\n        return {\n            \"accuracy\": accuracy,\n            \"bets_per_month\": bets_per_month,\n            \"sharpe\": sharpe,\n            \"max_drawdown\": max_drawdown,\n            \"total_return\": total_return,\n            \"n_trades\": len(self.trades),\n            \"profit_factor\": profit_factor,\n            \"final_equity\": self.equity_curve.iloc[-1] if self.equity_curve is not None else self.initial_capital,\n            \"gross_profit\": gross_profit,\n            \"gross_loss\": gross_loss\n        }\n\n    def get_equity_curve(self) -> pd.Series:\n        return self.equity_curve\n\n    def get_trades(self) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        return Create population (use only random_forest for speed and stability)\n            pop = Population(\n                population_size=5,  # small for horizon models\n                model_types=[\"random_forest\"],\n                elitism_count=1\n            )\n            pop.initialize(input_dim=len(feature_cols), feature_names=feature_cols)\n\n            # Quick training: fit all models\n            print(f\"    Training {len(pop.individuals)} models...\", flush=True)\n            self._train_population(pop, X, y)\n            self.horizon_models[horizon] = pop\n            print(f\"    Completed horizon {horizon}\", flush=True)\n\n        self.is_fitted = True\n        print(\"All horizon ensembles fitted\", flush=True)","originalFile":"\"\"\"\nHorizon ensemble: Integrate multi-horizon contextual signals.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\nfrom typing import Dict, List\nfrom ..models.population import Individual, Population\n\n\nclass HorizonEnsemble:\n    \"\"\"\n    Ensemble that uses multiple horizon-specific models as contextual features.\n\n    For each horizon (1h, 4h, 8h, 12h, 24h), we train a model on resampled data\n    and use its prediction as a feature for the main 15m model.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, horizons: List[str] = None, population: Population = None):\n        self.horizons = horizons or [\"1h\", \"4h\", \"8h\", \"12h\", \"24h\"]\n        self.population = population\n        self.horizon_models: Dict[str, Population] = {}  # horizon -> population\n        self.main_features = []  # list of feature names produced by this ensemble\n        self.is_fitted = False\n\n    def fit_horizon_models(\n        self,\n        horizon_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame],\n        target_series: pd.Series\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Train a separate model population for each horizon.\n\n        Args:\n            horizon_data: dict of {horizon: DataFrame} with features for that horizon\n            target_series: target series (same for all, aligned to base timeframe)\n        \"\"\"\n        self.horizon_models = {}\n\n        horizons = list(horizon_data.items())\n        print(f\"Fitting horizon ensembles for {len(horizons)} horizons\", flush=True)\n        for idx, (horizon, df) in enumerate(horizons):\n            if df.empty:\n                print(f\"  Horizon {horizon}: empty, skipping\", flush=True)\n                continue\n\n            print(f\"  Horizon {horizon} ({idx+1}/{len(horizons)}): processing {len(df)} rows\", flush=True)\n            # Align target to this horizon's timestamps\n            df_indexed = df.set_index(\"open_time\")\n            target_aligned = target_series.reindex(df_indexed.index)\n\n            # Prepare features (exclude time and target columns)\n            feature_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c not in [\"open_time\", \"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\"]]\n            X = df[feature_cols]\n            print(f\"    Feature count: {len(feature_cols)}\", flush=True)\n\n            # Create population (use only random_forest for speed and stability)\n            pop = Population(\n                population_size=5,  # small for horizon models\n                model_types=[\"random_forest\"],\n                elitism_count=1\n            )\n            pop.initialize(input_dim=len(feature_cols), feature_names=feature_cols)\n\n            # Quick training: just fit all models (simplified, should be evolutionary)\n            print(f\"    Training {len(pop.individuals)} models...\", flush=True)\n            self._train_population(pop, X, target_aligned)\n            self.horizon_models[horizon] = pop\n            print(f\"    Completed horizon {horizon}\", flush=True)\n\n        self.is_fitted = True\n        print(\"All horizon ensembles fitted\", flush=True)\n\n    def _train_population(self, population: Population, X: pd.DataFrame, y: pd.Series):\n        \"\"\"Fit all models in a population.\"\"\"\n        total = len(population.individuals)\n        for i, ind in enumerate(population.individuals):\n            try:\n                print(f\"[horizon]   Training model {i+1}/{total} ({ind.model_type})...\", flush=True)\n                ind.model.fit(X, y)\n                print(f\"[horizon]   Model {i+1} trained\", flush=True)\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"[horizon]   Model {i+1} failed: {e}\", flush=True)\n                # Set very low fitness if model fails\n                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n    def generate_features(\n        self,\n        horizon_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame],\n        base_index: pd.DatetimeIndex\n    ) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate horizon ensemble features: predictions from each horizon's best model.\n\n        Returns:\n            DataFrame with columns like: h_1h_pred, h_1h_proba, h_4h_pred, ...\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self.is_fitted:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"HorizonEnsemble must be fitted first\")\n\n        features = pd.DataFrame(index=base_index)\n\n        for horizon, pop in self.horizon_models.items():\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n\n            # Get horizon data aligned to base index\n            df_h = horizon_data[horizon].set_index(\"open_time\")\n            X_h = df_h.reindex(base_index)\n\n            # Drop non-feature columns\n            feature_cols = [c for c in X_h.columns if c not in [\"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\", \"quote_volume\"]]\n            X_h_features = X_h[feature_cols]\n\n            # Forward fill any missing features (from resampling gaps)\n            X_h_features = X_h_features.ffill()\n\n            # Generate predictions\n            try:\n                proba = best.model.predict_proba(X_h_features)\n                pred = best.model.predict(X_h_features, threshold=0.5)\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_proba\"] = proba\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_pred\"] = pred\n            except Exception as e:\n                # If prediction fails, fill with 0.5 (neutral)\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_proba\"] = 0.5\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_pred\"] = 0\n\n        self.main_features = list(features.columns)\n        return features\n\n    def get_feature_names(self) -> List[str]:\n        return self.main_features\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":26,"oldLines":14,"newStart":26,"newLines":14,"lines":["     def fit_horizon_models(","         self,","         horizon_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame],","-        target_series: pd.Series","+        target_series: pd.Series = None","     ):","         \"\"\"","         Train a separate model population for each horizon."," ","         Args:","-            horizon_data: dict of {horizon: DataFrame} with features for that horizon","-            target_series: target series (same for all, aligned to base timeframe)","+            horizon_data: dict of {horizon: DataFrame} with features and 'target' column for that horizon","+            target_series: ignored (for backward compatibility)","         \"\"\"","         self.horizon_models = {}"," "]},{"oldStart":45,"oldLines":15,"newStart":45,"newLines":31,"lines":["                 continue"," ","             print(f\"  Horizon {horizon} ({idx+1}/{len(horizons)}): processing {len(df)} rows\", flush=True)","-            # Fetch latest data\n2. Generate features (including horizon ensembles)\n3. Evolve model population\n4. Train best models with walk-forward validation\n5. Generate signals via consensus gating\n6. Paper trade and measure performance\n7. Reflect on metrics and adjust system\n8. Repeat\n\"\"\"\n\nimport os\nimport sys\nimport json\nimport pickle\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Dict, Generator, Tuple, Optional\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\n\nfrom .config import *\nfrom .data.fetcher import BinanceDataFetcher, fetch_and_save_historical\nfrom .features.horizon import generate_horizon_features\nfrom import SimulatedTrader\nfrom .trading.metrics import evaluate_predictions\n\n\nclass TradingSystem:\n    \"\"\"\n    Main trading system class that orchestrates the full workflow.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        data_path: str = None,\n        results_dir: str = PathConfig.RESULTS_DIR,\n        log_dir: str = PathConfig.LOGS_DIR\n    ):\n        self.data_path = data_path or Path(PathConfig.DATA_DIR) / f\"{DataConfig.SYMBOL.lower()}_{DataConfig.INTERVAL}.csv\"\n        self.results_dir = Path(results_dir)\n        self.log_dir = Path(log_dir)\n\n        self.results_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        self.log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        # Current state\n        self.df = None\n        self.feature_names = []\n        self.main_population = None\n        self.horizon_ensemble = None\n        self.consensus_gate = None\n        self.validator = None\n        self.trader = None\n\n        # Load or initialize\n        self._init_components()\n\n    def _init_components(self):\n        \"\"\"Initialize all system components.\"\"\"\n        self.validator = WalkForwardValidator(\n            train_days=ValidationConfig.TRAIN_DAYS,\n            valid_days=ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS,\n            step_days=ValidationConfig.STEP_DAYS,\n            min_train_samples=ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES\n        )\n\n        self.trader = SimulatedTrader(\n            initial_capital=TradingConfig.INITIAL_CAPITAL,\n            fee_rate=TradingConfig.FEE_RATE,\n            slippage=TradingConfig.SLIPPAGE,\n            bet_size=TradingConfig.POSITION_SIZE\n        )\n\n        self.horizon_ensemble = HorizonEnsemble(horizons=FeatureConfig.HORIZONS)\n        self.consensus_gate = ConsensusGate(\n            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n        )\n\n    def load_or_fetch_data(self, force_refetch: bool = False) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load data from disk or fetch from Binance if not available/forced.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not force_refetch and self.data_path.exists():\n            print(f\"Loading data from {self.data_path}\", flush=True)\n            df = pd.read_csv(self.data_path, parse_dates=[\"open_time\"])\n        else:\n            print(\"Fetching fresh data from Binance...\", flush=True)\n            df = fetch_and_save_historical(\n                symbol=DataConfig.SYMBOL,\n                interval=DataConfig.INTERVAL,\n                start_date=DataConfig.START_DATE,\n                filepath=str(self.data_path)\n            )\n\n        if df.empty:\n            raise ValueError(\"No data available\")\n\n        print(f\"Loaded {len(df)} rows of data\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Date range: {df['open_time'].iloc[0]} to {df['open_time'].iloc[-1]}\", flush=True)\n        return df\n\n    def prepare_features(self, df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Prepare feature matrix X and target y.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Preparing features...\", flush=True)\n\n        # Clean data\n        df = clean_data(df)\n\n        # Create target (next 15-minute direction)\n        df = create_target(df, forward_periods=1)\n\n        # Generate horizon ensemble features (these will be added as extra columns)\n        print(\"  Generating horizon features...\", flush=True)\n        horizon_features = generate_horizon_features(df, FeatureConfig.HORIZONS)\n\n        # Merge horizon features into df\n        df = df.merge(horizon_features, on=\"open_time\", how=\"left\")\n\n        # Drop rows with NaN in target\n        df = df.dropna(subset=[\"target\"])\n\n        # Forward fill any remaining NaNs\n        df = df.ffill().dropna()\n\n        print(f\"Prepared {len(df)} samples with {len(df.columns)} columns\", flush=True)\n        self.feature_names = [\n            col for col in df.columns\n            if col not in [\n                \"open_time\", \"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\",\n                \"quote_volume\", \"trades_count\", \"future_close\", \"target\"\n            ]\n        ]\n\n        print(f\"Feature count: {len(self.feature_names)}\", flush=True)\n        return df\n\n    def evolve_population(\n        self,\n        X_train: pd.DataFrame,\n        y_train: pd.Series,\n        generations: int = ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION\n    ) -> Population:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evolve a population of models.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(f\"Evolving population (input_dim={X_train.shape[1]})...\", flush=True)\n\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(generations):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen + 1}/{generations}\", flush=True)\n\n            # Evaluate all individuals\n            for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\n                try:\n                    print(f\"    Training individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    proba = ind.model.predict_proba(X_train)\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    accuracy = (pred == y_train).mean()\n\n                    # Simple fitness based on training accuracy (we'll validate on walk-forward later)\n                    ind.fitness = accuracy\n                    print(f\"      -> fitness={ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"      -> training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\n            if gen < generations - 1:\n                print(\"  Evolving to next generation...\", flush=True)\n                pop.evolve()\n\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} (model={best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        return pop\n\n    def run_walk_forward(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame\n    ) -> Dict[str, float]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Run walk-forward validation with a single temporal split.\n        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\n        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\", flush=True)\n\n        df_sorted = df.sort_values(\"open_time\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        # Determine validation period length (e.g., 7 days)\n        val_days = ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS\n        val_rows = int(val_days * 24 * 60 / 15)  # number of 15-min candles\n\n        total_rows = len(df_sorted)\n        if total_rows < val_rows + 1000:\n            print(\"Insufficient data for walk-forward split.\", flush=True)\n            return {\"accuracy\": 0.0, \"bets_per_month\": 0.0, \"n_folds\": 0}\n\n        # Split: validation = most recent val_rows, training = older data\n        valid_df = df_sorted.iloc[-val_rows:].copy()\n        train_df = df_sorted.iloc[:-val_rows].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        print(f\"Train: {len(train_df)} samples, Validation: {len(valid_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n        X_valid = valid_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_valid = valid_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # Evolve a fresh population on the training set\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\", flush=True)\n            # Evaluate individuals on training data\n            for ind in pop.individuals:\n                try:\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    # Use training accuracy as fitness for selection\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"    Training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\n            if gen < ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION - 1:\n                print(\"  Evolving to next generation...\", flush=True)\n                pop.evolve()\n                best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n                print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n            else:\n                # Final generation: no evolution, just report best\n                best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n                print(f\"    Final best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        # Prepare close prices for simulation (used later and here)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)\n        print(\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\", flush=True)\n        for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\n            # Skip individuals that failed training\n            if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:\n                print(f\"  Skipping individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} (failed training)\", flush=True)\n                continue\n            try:\n                print(f\"  Rescoring individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n                # Predict on validation\n                pred_valid = ind.model.predict(X_valid, threshold=0.5)\n                # Ensure pred_valid is a Series with index\n                if isinstance(pred_valid, np.ndarray):\n                    pred_valid = pd.Series(pred_valid, index=X_valid.index)\n                # Create signals series aligned to close_prices (fill missing with -1)\n                signals_ind = pred_valid.reindex(close_prices.index).fillna(-1).astype(int)\n                # Run simulation\n                self.trader.run(signals_ind, close_prices)\n                v_metrics = self.trader.metrics\n                # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)\n                ind.fitness = v_metrics.get('sharpe', -1000)\n                print(f\"    -> Sharpe: {ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)\n                # Preserve validation metrics for later analysis if needed\n                ind.validation_metrics = v_metrics\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"  Validation rescoring error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n        # Get top diverse models based on validation Sharpe\n        top_models = pop.get_diverse_best(n=5)\n\n        # Generate consensus signals on validation set\n        consensus = ConsensusGate(\n            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n        )\n        signals = consensus.decide(top_models, X_valid)\n\n        # Align close prices (indices correspond)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Run simulator on signals\n        self.trader.run(signals, close_prices)\n        metrics = self.trader.metrics.copy()\n\n        # Compute raw accuracy\n        mask = (signals != -1) & y_valid.notna()\n        if mask.any():\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = (signals[mask] == y_valid[mask]).mean()\n        else:\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = 0.0\n\n        metrics[\"n_folds\"] = 1\n        print(f\"Walk-forward results: accuracy={metrics['accuracy']:.4f}, bets/month={metrics.get('bets_per_month', 0):.2f}, Sharpe={metrics.get('sharpe', 0):.4f}\", flush=True)\n\n        return metrics\n\n    def train_best_models(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame,\n        population: Population,\n        horizon_data: dict = None\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Train the best models on full recent data and optionally train horizon ensembles.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Training best models on full data...\", flush=True)\n\n        # Use recent data (last N days) for final training\n        recent_days = 60\n        cutoff_time = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=recent_days)\n        recent_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff_time].copy()\n        print(f\"Recent training data: {len(recent_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n\n        X_recent = recent_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_recent = recent_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # Get top models\n        top_models = population.get_diverse_best(n=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE + 2)\n        print(f\"Selected {len(top_models)} top models for retraining\", flush=True)\n\n        # Retrain on full recent data\n        for i, ind in enumerate(top_models):\n            try:\n                print(f\"  Retraining model {i+1}/{len(top_models)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n                ind.model.fit(X_recent, y_recent)\n                print(f\"    Done\", flush=True)\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"  Failed to train {ind.model_type}: {e}\", flush=True)\n\n        # Train horizon ensemble if not provided and if we have multiple horizons\n        if horizon_data is None:\n            horizon_data = {}\n            horizons_to_train = [h for h in FeatureConfig.HORIZONS.keys() if h != \"15m\"]\n            if horizons_to_train:\n                print(f\"Training horizon ensemble for {len(horizons_to_train)} horizons...\", flush=True)\n                for h_name in horizons_to_train:\n                    factor = FeatureConfig.HORIZONS[h_name]\n                    print(f\"  Horizon {h_name} (factor={factor})\", flush=True)\n                    from .data.processor import resample_to_horizon\n                    # Use horizon minutes correctly; but for speed we skip due to minimal HORIZONS\n                    df_horizon = resample_to_horizon(recent_df, recent_days, factor)\n                    print(f\"    Resampled to {len(df_horizon)} rows\", flush=True)\n                    if len(df_horizon) > 100:\n                        df_horizon = create_target(df_horizon, forward_periods=1)\n                        df_horizon = add_technical_indicators(df_horizon)\n                        df_horizon = add_regime_features(df_horizon)\n                        df_horizon = df_horizon.dropna()\n                        print(f\"    Prepared {len(df_horizon)} samples with {len(df_horizon.columns)} features\", flush=True)\n                        horizon_data[h_name] = df_horizon\n                self.horizon_ensemble.fit_horizon_models(horizon_data, y_recent)\n                print(\"Horizon ensemble training complete\", flush=True)\n            else:\n                print(\"No additional horizons to train (HORIZONS only includes 15m)\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            self.horizon_ensemble.fit_horizon_models(horizon_data, y_recent)\n\n        self.main_population = population\n        self.top_models = top_models\n\n    def generate_current_signals(\n        self,\n        current_features: pd.DataFrame,\n        use_horizon: bool = True\n    ) -> pd.Series:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate trading signals for the most recent data points.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.top_models is None:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"Models not trained. Call train_best_models first.\")\n\n        signals = self.consensus_gate.decide(self.top_models, current_features)\n        return signals\n\n    def run_online_step(self, new_data: pd.DataFrame = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute one iteration of the online evolution loop.\n\n        This is the main step method called repeatedly by Arbos.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n        print(\"RUNNING ONLINE STEP\", flush=True)\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n\n        # 1. Load/fetch latest data\n        if new_data is None:\n            df = self.load_or_fetch_data()\n        else:\n            df = new_data\n\n        # 2. For online operation, limit to most recent N days to bound runtime\n        if ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS is not None:\n            cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS)\n            df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Online mode: limited data to last {ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS} days ({len(df)} rows)\", flush=True)\n\n        # 3. Prepare features\n        print(\"[main] Starting feature preparation...\", flush=True)\n        df = self.prepare_features(df)\n        print(f\"[main] Feature preparation complete: {len(df)} rows, {len(self.feature_names)} features\", flush=True)\n\n        # 3. Split into recent data for training and current for prediction\n        # Use last 30 days for training, current for signal generation\n        recent_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=30)\n        train_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] <= recent_cutoff].copy()\n        current_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] > recent_cutoff].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to control memory/time (use most recent samples)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        if len(train_df) < ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            print(f\"Insufficient training data: {len(train_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n            return None\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # 4. Evolve population\n        population = self.evolve_population(X_train, y_train, generations=ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION)\n\n        # 5. Walk-forward validation on historical folds (use recent data to limit folds)\n        if ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS is not None:\n            wf_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS)\n            df_wf = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= wf_cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Using {len(df_wf)} recent samples for walk-forward validation (max {ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS} days)\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            df_wf = df\n        metrics = self.run_walk_forward(df_wf)\n\n        # 6. Train best models on full recent data and prepare horizon ensemble\n        self.train_best_models(df, population)\n\n        # 7. Generate current signals\n        if len(current_df) > 0:\n            current_features = current_df[self.feature_names]\n            signals = self.generate_current_signals(current_features)\n            print(f\"Generated {len(signals)} current signals\", flush=True)\n            latest_signal = signals.iloc[-1] if len(signals) > 0 else None\n            print(f\"Latest signal: {latest_signal} (1=UP, 0=DOWN, -1=NO TRADE)\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            signals = pd.Series()\n            latest_signal = None\n\n        # 8. Save results\n        self._save_results(metrics, population, signals)\n\n        # Compile full result\n        result = {\n            \"timestamp\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            \"accuracy\": metrics.get(\"accuracy\", 0.0),\n            \"bets_per_month\": metrics.get(\"bets_per_month\", 0.0),\n            \"sharpe\": metrics.get(\"sharpe\", 0.0),\n            \"max_drawdown\": metrics.get(\"max_drawdown\", 0.0),\n            \"total_return\": metrics.get(\"total_return\", 0.0),\n            \"latest_signal\": int(latest_signal) if latest_signal is not None else None,\n            \"population_fitness\": {str(i): ind.fitness for i, ind in enumerate(population.get_best(5))}\n        }\n\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n        print(\"STEP COMPLETE\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Metrics: {json.dumps(result, indent=2)}\", flush=True)\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n\n        return result\n\n    def _save_results(self, metrics: dict, population: Population, signals: pd.Series):\n        \"\"\"Save results to disk.\"\"\"\n        timestamp = datetime.utcnow().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n        run_dir = self.results_dir / timestamp\n        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        #      symbol=DataConfig.SYMBOL,\n                interval=DataConfig.INTERVAL,\n                start_date=DataConfig.START_DATE,\n                filepath=str(self.data_path)\n            )\n\n        if df.empty:\n            raise ValueError(\"No data available\")\n\n        print(f\"Loaded {len(df)} rows of data\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Date range: {df['open_time'].iloc[0]} to {df['open_time'].iloc[-1]}\", flush=True)\n        return df\n\n    def prepare_features(self, df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Prepare feature matrix X and target y.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Preparing features...\", flush=True)\n\n        # Clean data\n        df = clean_data(df)\n\n        # Create target (next 15-minute direction)\n        df = horizon_features = generate_horizon_features(df, FeatureConfig.HORIZONS)\n\n        # Merge horizon features into df\n        df = df.merge(horizon_features, on=\"open_time\", how=\"left\")\n\n        # Drop rows with NaN in target\n        df = df.dropna(subset=[\"target\"])\n\n        # Forward fill any remaining NaNs\n        df = df.ffill().dropna()\n\n        print(f\"Prepared {len(df)} samples with {len(df.columns)} columns\", flush=True)\n        self.feature_names = [\n            col for col in df.columns\n            if col not in [\n                \"open_time\", \"open\", (input_dim={X_train.shape[1]})...\", flush=True)\n\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n
